Name: EDC Lancaster County
Description:
EDC Lancaster County is the leading organization dedicated to promoting economic development in Lancaster County, Pennsylvania. A private, not-for-profit, non-government organization, EDC works on a number of fronts to promote the economic well-being of our communities.

Our team provides:
» Expert insights about the local economy & development process
» Connections to business & community leaders
» Access to funding programs that are tailored to help businesses of all sizes & industries that are looking to expand & grow.

Name: Virginia Black Chamber of Commerce
Description:
The mission of the Virginia Black Chamber of Commerce (VABCC) is to promote the sustainability of black-owned businesses throughout the Commonwealth of Virginia.
Together with the Virginia Black Business Directory (VABBD), our 501c3 organization we are committed to the capacity building of small business owners; by provide programs and initiatives that foster higher productivity, increased success rates, and improved business education.

Name: Maryland Clean Energy Center
Description: Funding and facilitating a clean energy future through the adoption of clean energy and climate mitigation products, services, and technologies in Maryland.

Name: The Other Economy
Description:
Fondée par Alain Grandjean et Marion Cohen, The Other Economy est une plateforme de connaissances et de propositions destinée à celles et ceux qui veulent agir pour transformer l’économie dans une direction socialement juste et écologiquement soutenable. 

Notre ambition : permettre à chacun de se doter d’outils pour se représenter le monde économique dans sa globalité et adopter une vision critique de ce qu’est l’économie ; des outils également pour se mobiliser en diffusant les idées qui permettront le renouveau de la pensée économique afin de répondre aux enjeux humains et naturels du XXIè siècle.

#climat #finance #écologie #environnement #pédagogie #économie #monnaie #dette #energie #travail #emploi #chomage

Name: Reform Secretariat | MoPIC
Description:
The Reform Secretariat was established in 2019 and housed within the Ministry of Planning and International Cooperation (MoPIC) to lead on supporting the implementation of the Reform Matrix which was launched during the London Initiative “Jordan: Growth and Opportunities”.

The Jordan's Reform Matrix comprises of a set of policy and structural reforms that aims at maintaining macroeconomic stability, improving business environment, and increasing investments and exports. Furthermore, it serves as a guidance document for the plans and programs of the Government on one hand, and donors and development partners on other hand. 

The Reform Secretariat is the focal point from the Government of Jordan side for the Jordan Inclusive Growth and Economic Opportunities Multi-Donor Trust Fund which is managed by the World Bank Group, Co-Chaired by Ministry of Planning and International Cooperation, and currently funded by the governments of United Kingdom, Netherlands, Canada, Germany and Norway.

Name: Lake to River Ohio
Description: Lake to River is dedicated to creating sustainable economic growth through site development, business attraction and retention, and strategic infrastructure enhancement. These initiatives uplift our region, integrating project management, talent acquisition, and marketing for community betterment. Join us in shaping a brighter future for Eastern Ohio.

Name: Medicine Hat Economic Development
Description: Medicine Hat Economic Development is energizing our future. Join us in creating a strong and resilient Medicine Hat.

Name: Transforma Cambio Climático
Description: Somos el programa de Corfo que busca conectar al sector productivo del país con el ecosistema climático.

Name: BEAM Circular
Description:
BEAM Circular is building a vibrant regional ecosystem for the circular bioeconomy in California’s agricultural heartland. Anchored in the North San Joaquin Valley, we are facilitating collaboration through the BioEconomy, Agriculture, & Manufacturing (BEAM) Initiative to scale the most promising innovations in bioindustrial manufacturing and to transform waste from food and agricultural systems into economic and environmental opportunity. Our work includes public-private projects and programs in the areas of innovation, access to capital, infrastructure, talent development, and cross-cutting partnerships to advance the sector.

BEAM Circular was launched in January 2023, catalyzed by the Stanislaus 2030 inclusive economic development partnership. Together with local communities and a growing network of state and national collaborators, BEAM Circular is now aligning resources, policy, talent, and collaboration to accelerate the circular bioeconomy and make the North San Joaquin Valley the best place in the world to bring bioproduction solutions to scale.

BEAM Circular serves as co-lead of the Circular Bioeconomy Innovation Collaborative (CBIO Collaborative), a NSF Engines Development Award recipient, alongside UC Merced and Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory.

Name: Pacific Islands Tax Administrators Association
Description:
PITAA was initiated in May 2003 during a seminar in Tonga which was attended by eight tax representative of the Pacific Island Countries (PICs). The seminar was organized by the Pacific Financial Technical Assistance Centre (PFTAC). 
This conference heralded the pledge by tax administrators to address tax issues most relevant to them and also promote closer cooperation and coordination, information sharing, promote international standards best tax practices, enhance skills and encourage development of high tax professionals. This was largely seen as the “initial step” towards strengthening their professional ties and discusses ways to formulate and adopt strategies to modernize the tax administration.
PITAA has now built an environment in which PICs tax administration annually meet to freely share their views and desires for their individual administration

Name: EnterpriseKC
Description:
EnterpriseKC is a business-led, systems driven, “think and do” tank focused on enterprise development, innovation, and job growth within high value, high opportunity industry clusters.

We work directly with business, academia, and government to understand current cluster ecosystems and to advance strategies and programs that result in accelerated enterprise growth and economic prosperity throughout Kansas and Western Missouri.

Name: DG "European Funds for Competitiveness" / ГД "Европейски фондове за конкурентоспособност"
Description:
Главна дирекция "Европейски фондове за конкурентоспособност" (ГД ЕФК) е една от четирите специализирани дирекции в Министерството на иновациите и растежа (МИР)

ГД ЕФК е Управляващ орган на Програмата за конкурентоспособност и иновации в предприятията 2021 - 2027  (ПКИП) - пряко насочена към постигането на интелигентен и устойчив растеж на българската икономика, както и осъществяването на индустриална и цифрова трансформация.

Name: Spoorzone Zwolle
Description: In het nieuwe gebied Spoorzone Zwolle komt onze geschiedenis, stad en platteland, het hier & nu én onze toekomst tot leven.

Name: CCIFA (Chambre de Commerce et d'Industrie Franco Arménienne)
Description:
La CCIFA a pour but de promouvoir les relations économiques entre la France et l’Arménie et sert de plate-forme pour favoriser les échanges entre leurs entreprises.
La Chambre offre à cet égard un réseau d’affaires utile aux deux communautés ainsi qu’un Club convivial permettant de tisser des liens amicaux aussi bien que professionnels.
Dans cet esprit, la Chambre organise diverses manifestations telles que des déjeuners-débats, des soirées,des séminaires, des événements, des voyages, l’accès privilégié à des rencontres officielles et la célèbre cérémonie de remise des trophées de la réussite qui récompense le monde des chefs d’entreprises,de l’économie et des célébrités franco-arméniennes.

Name: Helvetas Jordan
Description: Helvetas Swiss Intercooperation is a specialized organization in development cooperation and humanitarian response. We are dedicated to creating a just world where individuals determine their lives with dignity and security, while utilizing environmental resources sustainably.

Name: Catawba County Economic Development Corp.
Description:
The Catawba County Economic Development Corporation strives to build a diversified economic base throughout Catawba County by recruiting new business and industry, by retaining existing business and industry and helping them grow, and by fostering the creation of jobs. 

In short, the Catawba EDC is a catalyst for industrial development in NC and Catawba County specifically. 

Since 1978 the EDC has assisted in the creation of over 28,500 jobs and over $7.9 billion in investment in Catawba County. In the last five fiscal years (2017 - 2021), the EDC has assisted in the creation of over 3,200 jobs and nearly $2.7 billion of investment.

Name: Observatorio del Contexto Económico de la UDP (OCEC UDP)
Description:
El Observatorio del Contexto Económico de la Universidad Diego Portales (OCEC UDP), tiene como objetivo principal aportar herramientas teóricas y experiencia práctica para influir en la relación entre las empresas, el gobierno y la sociedad, siendo una fuerza innovadora en la investigación, la enseñanza y el servicio público.
El OCEC UDP está en la búsqueda permanente de contribuir a enriquecer el debate, aportando desde la evidencia y siempre con una actitud propositiva. Como parte de sus tareas, el Observatorio se encarga de difundir y entregar insumos valiosos para una mejor comprensión del entorno económico y para la realización de políticas públicas con sólidos fundamentos desde el punto de vista técnico, que sean eficaces en lograr un mayor bienestar social y compatibles con el desarrollo sustentable del país.
La entidad, que inició sus actividades en marzo de 2021, funciona al alero de la Facultad de Administración y economía de la Universidad Diego Portales.

Name: Communauté d'Agglomération des Sorgues du Comtat
Description:
Cette page est dédiée à l'information économique et au développement territorial des Sorgues du Comtat. 
Les équipes et le réseau de partenaires des Sorgues du Comtat agissent au quotidien pour accompagner les filières d'excellence, soutenir le commerce et l'artisanat, développer l'économie touristique, favoriser une agriculture diversifiée et de qualité. 
L'intercommunalité investit aussi pour la création de nouveaux espaces économiques, la dynamisation des centres villes , la transition écologique et numérique de l'ensemble des acteurs.
Communes de : Monteux - Sorgues - Pernes Les Fontaines - Althen des Paluds - Bédarrides

Name: Korikart
Description:
Korikart is in the business of selling high quality beauty products at affordable rates. We import high end cosmetic products from South Korea. The company selects the finest products that are most likely to sell in the Indian market from a rich catalogue of South Korean cosmetics.

These products are sold all over the world including USA, Thailand and other western nations. We are bringing these products to India for the very first time.

These products are now readily available in India through our online shopping website korikart.com. We have been in business for over 8 years and now have an in-depth understanding of the Indian market. We take our customers very seriously and value their feedback. This constructive approach towards business has allowed our company to evolve, grow and expand. We are dedicated to our craft because we wish to establish our own benchmarks which future cosmetic companies would follow. We import our beauty products from South Korea because the country has a strong reputation for manufacturing products that meet the highest quality standards. They run their beauty products through rigorous tests and only clear them when they have met the highest standards. This dedication and commitment has helped South Korea become a powerhouse in the global cosmetic industry. The phrase South Korean beauty product alone rings a thousand bells and customers have no hesitation in buying beauty products made by them.

Name: Cobranza del Triunfo
Description: Venezuela y sus aliados triunfaremos tras guerra multiforme. Cobraremos lo despojado durante siglos.

Name: Grassroots Economics
Description:
Grassroots Economics is a non-profit foundation that seeks to empower marginalized communities to take charge of their own livelihoods and economic future. We focus on community development through economic empowerment programs. Beneficiaries of our programs include small businesses and people living in informal settlements as well as rural areas. 

Our goal is to improve the lives of those who are most vulnerable.

Name: Genesee County Economic Development Center
Description:
The GCEDC is the primary economic development agency in Genesee County, a community connecting the Buffalo, NY and Rochester, NY markets with shovel-ready sites and career-focused workforce development.

The GCEDC’s mission is to facilitate local economic growth and development which fosters investment and job creation for the benefit of our residents and children.

We do this by offering financial assistance, real estate solutions, workforce development programming and placemaking options in order to build back local and regional manufacturing and by supporting the continued growth and success of our local businesses all across Genesee County.

Since 2002, this mission has led to over $2 billion of capital investment, over 500 projects, and over 5,000 pledged jobs.

These milestones are growing, with our STAMP Mega-Site attracting over $1 billion of announced investment and 700 announced jobs by Plug Power and Edwards Vacuum. Over 540 buildable acres, are available at STAMP with direct connections to infrastructure including a 600-megawatt substation delivering 100% renewable hydropower to our users.

Name: California Jobs First
Description: California Jobs First invests in key sectors to drive sustainable economic growth, innovation, and access to good-paying jobs.

Name: Lake Norman Economic Development
Description: Lake Norman Economic Development is responsible for improving the quality of life for residents through the expansion of the tax base by attracting new capital investment and creating new, highly skilled jobs for the towns of Cornelius, Davidson and Huntersville.

Name: Fhunded
Description: fhunded is a ecosystem project created by Lancashire County Council, dedicated to fostering collaboration and investment in tech and digital businesses in Lancashire. With a strategic focus on networking, fhunded brings together key stakeholders, including funders and founders, through curated events that facilitate meaningful connections. Leveraging strategic partnerships, fhunded creates an environment conducive to knowledge exchange and collaboration, inspiring innovation and growth. Complementing the networking events, fhunded offers insightful workshops on investment readiness, equipping entrepreneurs with the knowledge and skills necessary to attract funding. By encouraging investment in the local tech and digital sectors, fhunded plays a vital role in accelerating Lancashire's economic development and positioning it as a thriving hub for technological advancement.

Name: Somali Development and Reconstruction Bank
Description:
The Somalia Development and Reconstruction Bank (SDRB) is a national development bank dedicated to fostering economic recovery, growth, and sustainable development in Somalia. Established in 1968 and reconstituted in 2014, SDRB operates with a renewed mandate to catalyse investments, drive innovation, and support the private sector in unlocking Somalia’s vast economic potential.

SDRB focuses on key sectors such as agriculture, fisheries, renewable energy, infrastructure, and financial services, addressing critical gaps in access to finance and promoting inclusive growth. By leveraging Somalia’s unique resources—such as its abundant solar energy potential, extensive coastline, and vibrant digital economy—SDRB aims to transform challenges into opportunities for prosperity.

With a commitment to Sharia-compliant financing, SDRB offers tailored financial instruments, including credit guarantees, asset financing, and public-private partnerships (PPPs), to de-risk investments and attract private capital. The bank also prioritises capacity building, technical assistance, and climate-resilient projects to ensure long-term sustainability.

As a catalyst for development, SDRB collaborates with international partners, government agencies, and local stakeholders to drive economic transformation, create jobs, and improve livelihoods across Somalia. Together, we are building a brighter future for Somalia, one investment at a time.

Name: ACTION FOR DEVELOPMENT INITIATIVE (AFODI)
Description: An Organization that focuses on three thematic areas, Socio-economic policy advocacy, economic research and entrepreneurship training and skilling.

Name: پایگاه خبری منشور اقتصاد
Description:
جامعه متخصصین و اساتید "پایگاه خبری منشور اقتصاد" با هدف ایجاد ارتباطی پویا میان دانشگاه، صنعت و دولت تأسیس شده است تا با بهرهگیری از توان علمی و تخصصی ایرانیان عزیز در سراسر دنیا ، به حل مسائل کلان ملی و توسعه اقتصاد دانشبنیان کمک کند. این جامعه، که بر اساس ( مسئولیت اجتماعی و تعهد به پیشرفت ایران عزیز ) شکل گرفته و به صورت مستقل فعالیت میکند، تلاش دارد از طریق (  شبکهسازی علمی و تخصصی )، فضایی برای نوآوری باز و جمعسپاری ایجاد کند

این جامعه با هدف تقویت همکاری میان دانشگاهیان، صنعتگران و سیاستگذاران، بستر لازم را برای ارائه راهحلهای عملی به چالشهای کلان کشور فراهم میکند. همچنین، با تأکید بر( تأثیرگذاری اجتماعی و اقتصادی )، به تقویت حس همکاری و مسئولیتپذیری میان اساتید و پژوهشگران میپردازد و آنها را به مشارکت فعال در توسعه پایدار و حل مسائل واقعی جامعه تشویق میکند. یکی دیگر از وظایف کلیدی این جامعه، ( هماهنگسازی نیازهای ملی با ظرفیتهای علمی ) است که با اطلاعرسانی فرصتها و ایجاد بسترهای مناسب، امکان بهرهگیری مؤثر از توانمندیهای علمی دانشگاهیان را فراهم میسازد

"به توان جمعی ایمان داریم"
 این شعار، روح اصلی فعالیتهای جامعه متخصصین و اساتید "پایگاه خبری منشور اقتصاد" را بازتاب میدهد و همه متخصصان و اساتید کشور را به همکاری برای ساخت آیندهای روشنتر دعوت میکند

Name: Muwazana
Description:

Name: Exit by Choice
Description:
Are you an entrepreneur, owning a company with 1-15M EUR in revenue ?!  Then probably your business is your largest asset. And this is exactly why you might be at risk!!!

You should consider making an exit and diversify your wealth. However, we believe that this must be by choice, not by hazard, nor by force. 

However, much too often this is not the case, and business owners like YOU find themselves either caught unprepared by unsolicited buying offers, or frustrated and burned-out by too many operational problems they carried for years. Hence, they leave money on the table, or miss a once in a lifetime opportunity.

Therefore, we are on a mission to guide business owners strategically prepare their business and themselves for an investment or an exit.

Name: Technikzentrum Lübeck
Description:
The Technology Center Lübeck (TZL) is a joint venture of Lübeck-based industry to promote innovation, knowledge transfer and startups. 

Since 1986, we have been providing a cooperative working environment for innovative startups and established technology companies in Schleswig-Holstein. From our co-working space to business offices and room for laboratories or production – we allow startups and fledging companies to grow together with their business. 

More than 50,000 square meters of professional business premises are available in 20 buildings at 3 location at favorable rental conditions and with supplementary infrastructure and services. Furthermore, as a one-stop moderating agency for its tenants and project partners, the TZL fosters the collaboration between companies and universities, initiates and accompanies technology transfer projects and acts as an incubator for new business ideas. The TZL initiates and supports a large number of research and cooperation projects as a project and network partner in order to promote ideas and innovations across borders such as GATEWAY49 StartUp Accelerator, StartUp SH or Innovative Hochschule and INTERREG-projects like Bildungsregion (with Denmark), Fehmarn Belt Innovation (with Denmark), Baltic Sea.

The TZL is centrally located on the Science Campus Lübeck, which provides an excellent environment for innovative startups and companies. Hardly any other location has such a concentrated and connected research and science landscape in the fields of medical technology, biotechnology and information technology. 

Imprint: www.tzl.de/impressum
Data: www.tzl.de/datenschutz

Name: Vietnam: Asia's Rising Star
Description:
For more than 25 years, Vietnam has been one of the most dynamic countries in the world in terms of GDP growth, trade, investment, and other business metrics. The key question is whether or not the country can continue this growth for another 25 years. If it can, Vietnam will become a high-income country like South Korea and Taiwan, the original Tiger Economies of Asia.

Engaging in conversations about Vietnam’s future is challenging. Locals are often too focused on the present while foreigners are caught up in legacy and political issues of the past. The authors believed a new look at the future of Vietnam was necessary and Vietnam – Asia’s Rising Star is the result.

Name: Blue Institute
Description:
The Blue Institute aims to protect coastal and marine natural assets by converting them to financial capital and empowering communities to manage their long-term sustainability as effectively as possible.

The Blue Institute is founded on six core principles:

1. Transparent and verifiable science enables us to prove the long-term financial value of natural marine assets.

2. Nature can be converted to a new asset class around the market value of ecosystem services, releasing capital to both protect and preserve critical natural marine resources.

3. Innovations in emerging technology can be utilised to reduce the time and cost of natural capital data gathering, analysis and monitoring.

4. Meaningful community engagement is essential to the long-term sustainable stewardship of marine and coastal natural resources.

5. The development of blue natural capital works best when approached within a holistic Blue Economy approach, underpinned by targeted, sensitive capacity building of all relevant stakeholders.

6. Wherever possible, we look to work in partnership with existing agencies for mutual benefit and to amplify the potential for rapid action.

Name: Energy Innovation Center
Description:
Designed and equipped by corporate partners, this 6.6 acre urban complex will promote energy-sector research and innovation and create direct and deliberate bridges to job creation, entrepreneurship and urban economic revitalization. It is positioned in the heart of a historic neighborhood that is sandwiched between the downtown central business district and the campuses of the region’s major research universities. Because the Hill District has suffered more than fifty years of economic disenfranchisement, community partners are working to ensure that this Energy Innovation Center will become an instrument of urban economic revitalization.
 
The energy technology innovation cluster in the Pittsburgh region has a rich history and a promising future. World-class capacity exists here in areas ranging from carbon management to nuclear reactor design, wind turbine manufacturing, materials and sensors, electric power distribution and control, shale gas production, and advanced building systems.
 
Pittsburgh Gateways Corporation, the region’s corporations, research universities, economic development organizations, and the U.S. Department of Energy’s National Energy Technology Laboratory have collaborated to develop this new Center so that it will realize a vision for accelerating the Nation’s transformation of its energy systems to achieve improved national security, competitiveness, and environmental stewardship.
 
This Center will co-locate collaborative university-industry projects, proof-of-concept energy technology demonstration laboratories, an early-stage business incubator and targeted workforce training programs. Corporate partners will showcase new products and deploy advanced energy management systems. Finally, the work of this LEED Platinum Center will catalyze the development of the surrounding 42-acre community footprint using international best practices for energy-efficient neighborhoods.

Name: BITE-CON
Description:
BITE-CON Foundation, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, is a beacon in the digital era, committed to bridging the technological divide. We aim to immerse, educate, and introduce people from underrepresented and underserved communities to groundbreaking technologies, ensuring no individual is left behind in the digital evolution. 

With our annual BITE-CON Conference, nationwide pop-up events, and community-based STEAM and esports activities, we engage and inspire, fostering dialogue about the transformative power of technology. Our focus extends beyond fostering enthusiasts; we cultivate technologists. 

Through comprehensive workshops and coding school scholarships, we equip community members with marketable tech skills, demystifying the industry, and making it accessible to all. 

Our ambitious goal is to impact a pipeline of 1,000,000 BIPOC and women technologists over the next five years through strategic partnerships, workforce development and thought leadership, fostering a tech world reflective of our diverse society. Join us as we bridge the digital divide and shape a future where technology is truly for all.

BITE-CON Conference 2025 will be held on April 10-11, 2025 in Miami, FL 

Register now: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/bite-con-2025-tickets-1203701757359?aff=ebdssbdestsearch

Generational Health are those effects passed on genetically, epigenetically and environmentally, shaping humanity's evolutionary curve.

These effects are scientifically and systemically measurable. They represent a multi-trillion dollar opportunity for both the public and private sector growth.

Operating across industries, regions and partners, TOWER and its affiliates deploy expertise and capital to uncover economic value, investment and innovation within Generational Health and bring it to stakeholders across the globe.

Name: Earth Valley
Description:
Blijf op de hoogte van het laatste nieuws rondom Earth Valley: hét ecosysteem op de as Amersfoort - Utrecht. Earth Valley is een samenwerking tussen bedrijven, kennisinstellingen en overheden met als doel te werken aan innovatieve oplossingen voor maatschappelijke vraagstukken, waarbij (geo-)data technologie de cruciale rol speelt. Deze samenwerking stelt ons in staat een fundament te leggen om de juiste prioriteiten te kiezen en innovaties en investeringen mogelijk te maken. 

Earth Valley heeft ambities om samen te werken op maatschappelijke vraagstukken met behulp van Digital Twin technologie en is bezig met het leggen van verbindingen tussen bedrijven, overheden en kennisinstellingen op het gebied van de Human Capital Agenda en geodata uit verschillende sectoren die voor verschillende ruimtelijke opgave staan. Zo spelen in de energietransitie vraagstukken over het plaatsen van duurzame energie-opwek. Hoe komen we bij een duurzaam gebouwde omgeving, en op welke manieren maken we een stad klimaatbestendig. Hoe kunnen we het beste omgaan met hitte en droogte, en zorgen we ervoor dat biodiversiteit toeneemt? En hoe gaan we ons verplaatsen in de toekomst? 
 
Samen zetten we de regio Utrecht op de kaart als dé regio met innovatieve oplossingen voor ruimtelijke vraagstukken!

Name: Sensorica
Description:
Individuals and organisations use this environment to set in motion and contribute to crowd-based economic processes. They create or contribute to open and collaborative projects, they crowdsource resources and harness collective intelligence.  

Traditional companies  benefit from rapid innovation and 50% cost reduction for R&D. 

Entrepreneurs expect over 80% reduction of financial costs to launch a product on the market.

SENSORICA is committed to the design and deployment of intelligent, open sensors, and sensemaking systems, which allow our communities to optimize interactions with our physical environment and realize our full human potential. 

Our core technology is a highly sensitive, optical fiber-based displacement/force transducer. At this moment, we integrate our transducer into sensors, and further into instruments used in physiology for the study of dynamic mechanical properties of animal tissues and cells. We are now expanding into laser-based range measurement and chemical sensing, developing applications for local food systems.

The P3 Portal is a platform and pre-due diligence evaluation model that helps business leaders align their projects with government priorities and get them in front of private equity investors. The resulting public-private partnerships help MIB to accelerate, expand, and improve its defense efforts to protect the security of our nation. 

The P3 Portal is an independent program funded by MIB to advance MIB’s mission. It is developed and operated by CapZone, an independent third party.

Name: D-HUB Special Economic Zone
Description:
D-HUB SEZ is Indonesia’s 23rd officially designated Special Economic Zone—and the first focused on four future-oriented sectors: Healthcare, Education, Digital Technology, and the Creative Economy.

Strategically located in BSD City, just 30 minutes from Soekarno-Hatta International Airport, D-HUB SEZ is at the heart of Indonesia’s innovation corridor. Covering 60 hectares, this SEZ is designed to attract global investment through government-backed incentives, streamlined regulations, and cutting-edge infrastructure powered by technologies such as AI, IoT, and cloud computing.

The ecosystem is anchored by a knowledge-sharing hub and supported by a Biomedical Hub, leading tech campuses, and digital centers of excellence—fostering collaboration between academia, industry, and government.

D-HUB SEZ offers flexible commercial opportunities, including Powell Studio Lofts—Indonesia’s first SEZ-based studio lofts—and customizable commercial lots ideal for labs, offices, and healthcare or tech facilities.

Whether you're a global healthcare provider, R&D institution, creative studio, or tech company, D-HUB SEZ provides a dynamic platform to scale and innovate in one of Asia’s most promising markets.

D-HUB SEZ is more than a destination—it’s a catalyst for transformation, where bold ideas take shape and the future begins.

Name: Yaletown Business Improvement Association
Description:
We are an independent non-profit association representing 750+ commercial tenants and property owners in Yaletown. We aim to enhance our members' business opportunities by leading action to improve the community environment and promote Yaletown as the place to visit and do commerce.

Yaletown is a vibrant commercial & residential district with a diverse mix of world-class and award-winning boutiques, eateries and services. The Yaletown BIA has evolved with the area and is now a mature organization with a long list of accomplishments & accolades, and the capability of organizing top-tier international opportunities such as the Vancouver 2010 Winter Olympics street party and outstanding public events that attract thousands of shoppers and diners annually.

Since 1999, the Yaletown BIA continues to: 
1. Beautify Yaletown’s streets and buildings
2. Foster economic development via effective marketing
3. Reduce (and minimize) commercial property taxes
4. Improve street safety and areal security
5. Maintain heritage district presence

Yaletown has been branded as Vancouver's most sought-out district.

Name: Florida Atlantic Innovation and Business Development
Description:
Imagine, build, grow, expand and thrive with FAU’s Innovation and Business Development pipeline. 

FAU has the resources and guidance you need on your journey to turn ideas into success. FAU is not only a place where you can earn your degree, but it's also a major community and state resource to promote economic development and entrepreneurship. The university’s continuum flows from student ideation to faculty companies and inventions, to real-world, large-scale business. 

We’ve built the pipeline for your growth.

Name: Giyak Mishkawzid Shkagmikwe (GMS)
Description: We are the economic development corporation for Atikameksheng First Nation.  Our mandate is to create prosperity for the community through the support and development of business ventures that earn revenues and create economic opportunities and capacity for Atikameksheng, its members, and partners.

Name: Wellbeing Economy Alliance of Vermont (WEAV)
Description:
The Wellbeing Economy Alliance of Vermont (WEAV), formerly the Vermont Prosperity Project, is a partnership between the Vermont Sustainable Jobs Fund, UVM GUND Institute, Abundant Sun LLC, Vermont Businesses for Social Responsibility, the Wellbeing Economy Alliance (WEAll), and the Vermont Council on Rural Development.
Our vision is an economy where we meet the needs of all people within the means of a living planet. We recognize that we cannot solve the multiple and interconnected crises facing us all without transforming our current economic system. Inspired by the principles of ecological economics and the UN Sustainable Development goals, we have been exploring the growing global movement of communities, businesses, cities, and countries who are gathering around the Doughnut Economics framework, the Wellbeing Economy Alliance movement, the Circular Economy movement, and others like it.

Our purpose is to align the actions of public, private and non-profit organizations so that by 2035 Vermont’s social foundations are met for all, within the ecological ceiling of the planet; in essence, Vermont citizens self-report high levels of well-being and our state’s metrics are solidly “inside the doughnut”. 

To mobilize Vermont policy makers to solve complex crises (e.g., housing, income inequality, workforce development, climate change) by taking an interconnected systems approach to societal and economic change.

Name: Palestine Economic Development Corporation
Description: Palestine Economic Development Corporation is a Type B Texas economic development corporation established under the Development Corporation Act of 1979.  Currently our division is funded by ¼ cent sales tax revenue. Our mission is: To promote and facilitate responsible commercial and industrial business retention and growth that expands, diversifies, and strengthens the local tax base while enhancing local amenities and destination attractions throughout the city of Palestine. Our department, within the city of Palestine, oversees economic development, business recruitment, retention, and expansion. The PEDC Board, established by state law and appointed by the Mayor and City Council, provides governance and policy direction for economic development of Palestine, Texas. #GrowPalestine

Name: Michigan Global Talent Initiative
Description:
The Michigan Global Talent Initiative (MGTI) consists of multiple statewide strategies to attract, retain, and place global talent in Michigan's workforce. These strategies rely on collaboration with economic development organizations, industry and economic development agencies, chambers of commerce, community colleges and universities, private sector companies, and state and local entities. MGTI programming is aligned with the Growing Michigan Together Council's recommendations, and works to realize the State’s Sixty by 30 goal: increase the number of working-age adults with a skill certificate or college degree to 60% by 2030.

MGTI's strategies include:
• International student retention
• Entrepreneurial support
• Skilled immigrant integration
• Credential evaluation and upskilling support
• Immigrant student success at community colleges
• Digital attraction campaign
• Work-based, industry-aligned English language learning

Name: Союз экономистов Туркменистана
Description: Цель национального общественного объединения «Союз экономистов Туркменистана» – содействие инновационному социально-экономическому развитию страны. Более 30 лет объединяя профессионалов, стремящихся к развитию экономики страны, Союз экономистов зарекомендовал себя как надежный партнер для государства, бизнеса, общественных и международных организаций. Основные направления деятельности Союза включают научно-исследовательскую работу, участие в законотворчестве, организацию образовательных программ, предоставление методической и консультационной поддержки, а также сертификацию и стандартизацию в области экономики, финансов, бухгалтерского учета, оценочной и консалтинговой деятельности.

Name: The Enterprise Center at PathStone
Description:
Serving WNY (Buffalo, Rochester, and surrounding cities/counties) and Puerto Rico.
Hablamos español!

The Enterprise Center at PathStone is an affiliate of PathStone Corporation, a multi-state community development corporation. Established in 1997, the Enterprise Center is a designated Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI).

Our mission is to enhance the economic self-sufficiency and quality of life of individuals and communities through entrepreneurial training, technical assistance, access to financing for new and expanding businesses, and mortgage products for underserved borrowers.

Our small business loans are paired with a strong program of pre-loan and post-loan technical assistance to support our clients' growth. While our loans range in size from $500 to $250,000, most start-ups are limited to a $50,000 maximum loan.

Name: Global Brain Economy Initiative
Description: Brain capital—the combination of brain health and brain skills—is essential to innovation, resilience, and economic growth in the 21st century. Yet significant gaps remain across healthcare systems, employers, education institutions, and governments in how these priorities are researched, supported, and translated into policy and practice. The Global Brain Economy Initiative (GBEI) addresses these gaps by bridging silos and advancing the brain economy through a central entity that serves as a driver, thought leader, and connective tissue across global efforts.

Name: GREENSMES - Interreg IPA Adrion
Description:
Enhancing SMEs’ modernization and green innovation and transition towards successful sustainability by transnational collaborative practices and tools.

Programme: Interreg IPA Adrion 2021-2027.
Programme priority: Supporting a smarter Adriatic - Ionian region
Lead partner: TECNOPOLIS Science & Technology Park (Italy)

The GREENSMES project addresses the challenge of promoting green innovation in business within the IPA ADRION territory, aiming to strengthen innovation capacities through transnational synergies of the quadruple helix. The project supports the green transition, clean technologies, SME development, skills enhancement, innovation ecosystem building, and rural development. Through knowledge exchange among partner regions and capacity building for SMEs, the project contributes to the Sustainable Development Goals and impactful investments in green innovations. Its outcomes include a strategy for improved cooperation between the R&D sector and the business community, as well as an action plan for the transfer and replication of activities.

Name: Street Economics
Description:
Street Economics lets you access BusinessFlare as your own personal economic development advisor for an affordable price.

At Street Economics, our mission is to empower cities with actionable insights that drive sustainable economic growth and enhance quality of life. By combining cutting-edge AI technology with decades of real-world expertise, we transform complex information into clear economic insights, helping communities uncover their potential, seize opportunities, and shape a brighter future for all.

Street Economics stands apart by combining cutting-edge AI with 30+ years of hands-on, real-world economic development experience through the BusinessFlare Approach. Our platform transforms municipal data into clear, actionable insights, enabling cities to make smarter decisions quickly. Unlike traditional consultants or generic data platforms, Street Economics offers:

Realistic, Attainable Solutions: We focus on actionable strategies that reflect your city’s unique challenges and potential, skipping the academic jargon and overwhelming data.

Customizable Insights: Tailored reports and comparisons empower decision-makers with the tools they need to identify opportunities and validate growth strategies.

Practical Expertise: Grounded in real-world experience, our platform provides insights that are immediately applicable to on-the-ground implementation.

Time-Efficient Results: With instant access to insights and continuously updated models, cities save time and resources while driving meaningful impact.

Street Economics is the trusted partner for cities seeking realistic, data-backed growth strategies without the status quo constraints.

Name: Billinda
Description: We all have certain items in our homes that we use once in a while, such as clothing or sporting gear that our kids have outgrown. These things occasionally wind up in storage, in the back of a closet, or, worst scenario, in a landfill. You can start a community on Billinda or join one that already exists to share your things or rent/buy from your neighbours. Everything that's shared has value to someone in some way.

Name: Conférence Daniel Cohen
Description: La conférence interroge chaque année un thème d'actualité à la lumière des travaux récents de la recherche en sciences économiques, et au prisme de ses dissonances apparentes avec le paradigme de la croissance économique. En croisant les regards de chercheurs, d'économistes institutionnels et de décideurs, nous poursuivons ainsi la traversée entamée par Daniel Cohen des sources de la croissance aux questions économiques contemporaines. Ce rendez-vous annuel réunit des économistes travaillant en lien avec la thématique annuelle et leur confie la tâche de vulgariser les enjeux de leur recherche et de leurs travaux.

Name: Wolfforth Economic Development Corporation
Description: The City of Wolfforth's Economic Development's purpose is to improve the quality of life for residents and the business community of Wolfforth, Texas through the recruitment and retention of targeted businesses that will increase property value and sales tax within the city.

Name: PharmaForum

Das PharmaForum ist ein jährlich stattfindendes Veranstaltungsformat zu aktuellen Themen der Pharma- und Gesundheitsbranche. Unter dem Motto „Pharma trifft Medizintechnik“ kommen Vertreterinnen und Vertreter der Wirtschaft, der Wissenschaft und der Politik zusammen, um sich gemeinsam zu informieren, zu diskutieren und sich zu vernetzen. Zudem bietet eine angegliederte Ausstellung Unternehmen die Möglichkeit, ihre Innovationen und Dienstleistungen direkt vor Ort zu präsentieren. Veranstaltet wird das PharmaForum vom Verband forschender Pharma-Unternehmen (vfa), vom
Gesundheitspolitischen Arbeitskreis Mitte als regionale Interessengemeinschaft forschender Pharma-Unternehmen sowie den Wirtschaftsministerien aus Hessen, Rheinland-Pfalz und dem Saarland.

The EU4Business—Trade, Investment, and Standards is a comprehensive project funded by the European Union in partnership with the Ministry of National Economy. It aims to expand and diversify Palestinian international trade, attract quality domestic and foreign investment, and enhance standards and quality infrastructure. 
The Project, carried out by CESO Development Consultants, Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH, and Business Integrated Solutions, seeks to foster revenue generation, create new job opportunities, spur innovation, and contribute to overall growth and welfare enhancement within the region.

Description: Wir stärken die Wirtschaftsstandorte in der Region Nordostniedersachsen als Teil der öffentlichen Wirtschaftsförderung, insbesondere fördern wir den Wissens- und Technologietransfer in der Region, erkennen innovative Themen und Potentiale und initiieren dafür die Entwicklung und Umsetzung von Projekten, wir koordinieren und bündeln regionale Innovationsakteure und tragen dazu bei, die Standortbedingungen für und die Innovationskraft von kleinen und mittleren Unternehmen zu stärken.

Name: Brain Economy Hub
Description:
The Brain Economy Hub’s mission is to pioneer and advance the brain economy, aka brain-positive economic transformation, into global economic discourse and activities. The intent is to stop and reverse the global loss of brain capital. 

The Hub is a global coordination and leadership hub for the brain economy. Project and personnel resources function in three key areas:
1) Ecosystem: Convening, alliance, dialogue, and lobbying
2) Technical: Data, research, and policy recommendations
3) Innovation: Innovation and investment

Name: The Economist Club
Description: Klubumuza xoş gəlmişsiniz. Klubumuz Xəzər Universitetinin İqtisadiyyat və Menecment fakültəsinin təşəbbüsü ilə yaradılmış, “The Economist Club” olaraq adlandırılmışdır. Klubumuzun məqsədləri sırasında Xəzər universitetinin iqtisadiyyata yönümlü tələbələrini  inkişaf etdirmək və onlara karyeralarında doğru yolu bulmağa kömək etməkdir. Klubumuzun saytı mövcuddur. Hansı ki, biz burada gənc iqtisadçıların elmi araşdırmalarını, Xəzər universitetində keçirilən təlimləri, debatları, elmi diskussiyaları, master klassları və digər inkişaf tədbirlərini sizlərlə bölüşürük.

Name: Zero to Traction
Description:
Startups are hard AF, risking your time, money, and reputation — and yet you’ve heard the call. You’re in. Now what?

In the Zero to Traction podcast, we tackle the major challenges faced by first-time founders building scalable startups.

Each episode provides tools, tips, stories, and mindset shifts to empower founders to overcome these obstacles to create companies that scale.

Description:
Nisg̱a’a leaders and citizens acknowledge the need to adapt with the changing needs of the Nation and Nisg̱a’a citizens and they recently embarked on a significant journey to re-envision and re-define the meaning of economic prosperity. The result was the Wilp Sìayuukhl Nisg̱a’a (WSN) Economic Prosperity Strategic Plan (EPSP) that identifies the vast potential within the Nation’s natural, cultural, human and financial resources. Nisg̱a’a Nation Vision

Name: Black Economic Success Trust (B.E.S.T)
Description:
B.E.S.T formerly MRBES, 501(c)(3), is a non-profit organization. Our mission is to minimize the traumatic effects of racism in BIPOC communities by strategically leveraging partnerships in an effort to dismantle systemic barriers that impede building wealth.

Name: GEA:GenZ Econ Alliance
Description:
We are 'GenZ Econ Alliance', a pioneering economics club initiated by high school students from City Montessori School, Kanpur Road Branch.We are not just a club but also a movement with a simple yet powerful mission: to shatter the stereotypes that often surround social studies subjects, proving that economics, entrepreneurship, finance, management, and leadership are not just theoretical concepts but practical tools for building a brighter future.

We believe in the potential of young minds to shape the world. That's why we've set out to build a vibrant and inclusive community where students can learn, grow, and think beyond the walls of the classroom. Our vision is to equip the next generation with the skills, knowledge, and mindset needed to succeed in a rapidly changing world.

At GEA, we stand united by a powerful belief: that the youth of today, the GenZ generation, holds the key to driving transformative change. Why do we believe this with unwavering conviction? Because we are all GenZ ourselves. We understand the challenges, aspirations, and dynamic perspectives that define our generation. We don't just talk about change; we embody it in everything we do.

In our GenZ community, we don't just learn; we inspire each other. We don't just dream; we take action. We don't just discuss change; we drive it.Join our journey, because the change we seek starts with us. Whether you're a fellow GenZ student or an industry professional who believes in the power of youth, GEA welcomes you to our movement. Together, let's break down barriers, challenge norms, and redefine the world of economics. We don't just talk about change; we are the change-makers.

Name: Oari | Oasis Rise Group
Description:
Oasis Rise Group (OARI) is a leading consultancy and agency based in Dubai, dedicated to enhancing UAE-Israel trade and economic cooperation. Established in the wake of the Abraham Accords, we specialize in bridging economic and cultural divides through strategic insights, comprehensive market support, and a robust network of partnerships.

At OARI GROUP, we offer a diverse range of services tailored to support Israeli businesses entering or expanding within the UAE market:

• Market Research & Insights: Providing customized market intelligence and sector reports to guide effective market entry strategies.
• Legal and Regulatory Guidance: Assisting with business setup, regulatory navigation, and compliance in the UAE.
• Networking & B2B Matchmaking: Connecting businesses with potential partners, investors, and government officials through trade delegations, forums, and investor relations.
• Governmental Facilitation: Navigating governmental processes and implementing agreements under the Abraham Accords.
• Sector-Specific Initiatives: Supporting innovation in technology, agriculture, clean energy, and more.
• Cultural and Economic Integration: Promoting mutual understanding and partnerships through cultural awareness programs and bilateral tourism initiatives.

Our mission is to empower businesses with the tools they need to succeed, fostering sustainable growth and meaningful partnerships between the UAE and Israel. With a structured approach and a commitment to excellence, OARI is your trusted partner for navigating the dynamic Middle Eastern market.

Join us in building bridges of opportunity and collaboration.

Name: COHAP OUTREACH CORPORATION
Description:
The COHAP Outreach Corporation is a 501c3 non-profit program that provides veterans, formerly incarcerated foster care young adults and formerly incarcerated juveniles with planning and prevention services. For those who are formerly incarcerated, young adults that are age out of foster care that are homeless and needs assistance because they are put out on the streets when they turn 18, and young teenagers who are in jail, which is called juvenile, or are about to go free, we offer a direct plan. The organization is composed of a team of professional advisors who are capable of meeting each client's needs. An evaluation and interview to establish their needs will be part of the programs client intake process.  Cohap is offering temporary housing aide, economic job skills, and financial literacy and home buying programs to prevent homelessnes. To serve as a foundation for success, Cohap Outreach Corporation works with agencies, educational institutions, and local nongovernmental organizations to provide certified educational programs, GED, vocational training, resume and interview skills, support groups for behavior and addiction issues, basic skills, mental health wellness, and financial literacy. 

 The main objective of the Organization shall be to support and improve these disadvantaged populations, so as to ensure that they are provided with adequate assistance in order to lead a fulfilled life and become active participants in society. Cohap Outreach Corporation aims to tackle the specific challenges and obstacles that each group faces, in order to stimulate their individual growth, rehabilitation or successful reintegration with their community under our programs.
 
For Veterans:
 The corporation salutes the great sacrifices made by the brave veterans who have sacrificed themselves in service of their country. The program offers a range of services to assist veterans in their transition from military life into civil life, which provide them with the necessary, psychological and emotional support. Cohap provides a full range of mental health services including access to counseling with managing complex bureaucratic hurdles relating to veterans' benefits.
 
For Formerly Incarcerated:
The corporation believes in the power of rehabilitation and second chances. The program is aimed at supporting persons who have been incarcerated to achieve a successful return to society. Cohap Outreach Corporation is providing a wide range of reentry services including job training, employment placement and assistance in securing permanent housing. The program supports formerly incarcerated to make better choices in their lives, strengthen relationships and become active members of society by way of mentorship programs and life skills training. They also prioritize education and encourage continued learning, equipping participants with the necessary tools to break the cycle of reoffending and lead law-abiding lives.

For Foster Care Young Adults and Formerly Incarcerated Juveniles:
Cohap understands the unique challenges faced by children and youths in the foster care system. The program also aims at providing a safe and supportive environment, in which these children can achieve their potential. Offering a range of comprehensive services, which include educational support, mental health counselling and the opportunity to engage in various activities promoting personal growth and development. Building strong networks of support, connecting foster children and young people with mentors and positive role models who can guide them through difficult life transitions, is also part of the programs. By offering stability, love, and opportunities for growth, Cohap aims to empower these young individuals to overcome adversity and reach their full potential.

 The programs aims at creating a society that supports and uplifts the people who have been affected by adversity. Cohap Outreach Corporation aims to provide them with the tools and resources necessary for a brighter future, to foster positive change and to break the cycle of poverty, by addressing the specific needs of veterans, former incarcerated and foster care young adults and juveniles.

Name: Afrikan Liberation Hub
Description:
In 2023, changemakers in Johannesburg and Cape Town engaged in a robust dialogue on economic systems change, and social and climate justice initiatives on the continent at the Afrikan Liberation Dinners by the Makers Valley Partnership and Wellbeing Economy Alliance. One resounding request from the dinners was to have space for continued collective engagements. In response, the Afrikan Liberation Hub emerges with the aim to be the beating heart of a movement based in Southern Afrika that unites, amplifies, and catalyses change and collaboration across the continent.

Our vision is to see Afrika liberated from colonial fingerprints and oppression, guided by indigenous values and dancing to the rhythm of collective prosperity. 

Our purpose is to foster unity, amplify voices, and ignite change by creating a space where diverse voices harmonise and the flames of transformation are ignited. 

Our mission is to co-create pathways toward Afrika's future that echo melodies of Afrikan liberation, anti-colonial justice, and decolonised wellbeing.

Name: Circulaire Fabrieken
Description:
Maakbedrijven die deelnemen aan het project 'Circulaire Fabrieken' gaan ambities stapsgewijs omzetten in een concreet circulair businessplan, onder andere door gebruik te maken van de kennis, het netwerk en de diverse technische en business tools die via het project ter beschikking worden gesteld. Denk aan vouchers voor inkoop van expertise of voor het afsluiten van innovatiecontacten, hulp bij matchmaking of ketensamenwerking, het oplossen van specifieke challenges e.d.

Voor een aantal plannen die market- en investment ready zijn is een bijdrage uit het Stimuleringsfonds Circulaire Fabrieken beschikbaar.

Circulaire Fabrieken is voor maakbedrijven gevestigd in de Cleantech Regio
- met impactvolle grondstoffenstromen in de levensmiddelenindustrie/ voedselverwerking, biomassaverwerking, bouw gerelateerde activiteiten, high-tech/apparatenbouw, papier- en kartonindustrie
- met aantoonbare ambitie en commitment van management (bijvoorbeeld opgenomen in bedrijfsvisie of -plan) en interne kennis over circulariteit
- met een status op de R-ladder
- reeds samenwerkend met de keten of op zoek naar samenwerking.

50 bedrijven in de Cleantech Regio nodigen wij op basis van dit profiel én aanbevelingen vanuit het netwerk persoonlijk uit om een quickscan uit te voeren. 

De resultaten uit de scan zijn basis voor advies over vervolgstappen, binnen of buiten het project.

Het project staat daarnaast open voor bedrijven die al een vergevorderd circulair businessplan hebben of investment ready zijn. 

Projectuitvoering: KplusV, Saxion Hogeschool en VNO-NCW Midden

Name: Finappie
Description:
Finappie je online ekonomický systém pre malé firmy a živnostníkov.

Finappie vám ušetrí čas a zníži administratívnu záťaž automatizáciou bežných procesov. Poskytuje prehľady a štatistiky, ktoré pomáhajú mať vždy prehľad o svojom cash flow, lepšie rozumieť finančnému stavu a rozhodovať sa objektívne na základe dát. Vďaka plánovaniu uvidíte koľko peňazí budete mať na vašich účtoch v ľubovoľný deň v najbližšom roku. Pomáha predchádzať upomienkam a pokutám z omeškania.

Finappie obsahuje fakturačný systém, prepojenie s bankovými účtami, párovanie dokladov a bločkov s transakciami z banky, prehľady budúcich aj minulých príjmov a výdajov, vytváranie cash flow plánu na 12+ mesiacov, pripomienky nadchádzajúcich platieb, aj exporty pre účtovníčku, ktoré plne nahrádzajú výpisy z bankových účtov.

Description:
Greater Nelson Economic Development (GNED) is a highly-effective collaboration between the City of Nelson, Regional District of Central Kootenay Areas E & F, Nelson & District Chamber of Commerce and Community Futures Central Kootenay. 

Since 2005, we've been working together to foster economic growth and address the unique needs of our community in the Nelson area. 

Our strength lies in our coordinated approach and strong relationships. We are able to streamline our efforts in pursuing local economic development opportunities, unite to solve challenges and drive growth. As a partnership, we are efficient and effective.

Description:
Descubre FECOPLA, el Foro Empresarial de la Economía Plateada, una empresa sin ánimo de lucro comprometida con el impulso y desarrollo sostenible del sector de la #EconomíaPlateada.

Nuestro objetivo es claro: crear, articular y hacer crecer este sector en el ámbito local, estatal, europeo y latinoamericano, beneficiando a toda la sociedad en el nuevo escenario del cambio demográfico.

Actuamos como un nodo de conexión entre empresas del sector, administraciones públicas y el tejido asociativo de personas mayores. 

Nuestra labor se centra en promover un enfoque inclusivo del envejecimiento activo y saludable, fomentando la cooperación y abordando desafíos críticos como la transformación digital y la sostenibilidad.

En FECOPLA nos comprometemos a combatir el #edadismo, derribando barreras para el emprendimiento en este sector en pleno auge. 

Contamos con la participación de empresas líderes como Bayparker Consulting, Digital Tech Consulting y Health and Aging Tech, dedicadas a impulsar soluciones innovadoras para mejorar la calidad de vida de las personas mayores.

Si deseas formar parte de esta revolución en la #SilverEconomy y contribuir al envejecimiento activo y saludable, te invitamos a unirte a FECOPLA! Visita nuestra web o contáctanos por mensaje privado.

#Longevidad #EconomíaPlateada #EnvejecimientoActivo #Emprendimiento

Name: Association for the Promotion of Open Society (APOS)
Description:
The “Association for the Promotion of Open Society” (APOS) was established in 2015 in Germany with the aim of expanding and strengthening the culture of tolerance, freedom and democracy. The fundamental goals of this association are developing human rights and liberal culture in order to overcome barriers on expanding open society. The APOS is mainly working about political, social and cultural changes in Iran. We are also interested about the integration process of migrants and iranian diaspora in Germany.

Mission Statement
 

The development of a society in different fields depends on various factors. By accurate and scientifically evaluating of these parameters, one can find facts that, on the one hand, indicate problems and failures and, on the other hand, provide the conditions necessary for the transition to a better society. This collection of human science and information, in fact, is a specialized and tested process in different societies that can in many ways be used as a model for development, especially in Third World countries.

Our mission is based on three points. First, we seek to establish and strengthen relations with intellectuals, especially those from the Middle East, North Africa and Europe, with a particular focus on Iranian issues. Second, we examine indicators such as “quality of governance” in the Islamic Republic of Iran, money laundering and corruption in various fields to promote public knowledge in these cases as well as to explain the benefits of free and democratic society to public opinion. Finally, we also try to increase specialized knowledge in areas such as promoting democratic dialogue, peace, security, and increasing citizen self-awareness. Many of its members are immigrants residing in Europe. Hence, we are also interested in theoretical and practical works in the field of immigrant’s integration in Europe. In order to achieve its goals and maximize the international community’s efforts to democratize, consolidate democratic

Name: Brunei Economic Development Board
Description:
The Brunei Economic Development Board (BEDB) envisions a resilient and diversified economy and is committed to catalysing sustainable growth by attracting and facilitating impactful investments that create jobs and generate opportunities for local enterprises.

BEDB also provides effective support and resources to enterprises of all sizes to spur innovation and growth, and develops fit-for-purpose industrial infrastructure to enable enterprises to thrive in a conducive environment. Focused on three strategic thrusts—Enabling Private Sector Growth, Increasing Internationalisation, and Enhancing BEDB Capabilities—BEDB is dedicated to driving economic development and fostering a vibrant business ecosystem in Brunei.

Name: GROW Tulare County
Description:
GROW Tulare County is the County of Tulare’s official economic development initiative, focused on advancing business growth, tourism, and quality of life across more than 40 unincorporated communities in Central California.

Housed within the County’s Resource Management Agency (RMA), our team provides direct assistance to businesses and investors seeking to locate or expand in Tulare County—offering end-to-end support from site selection to project completion.

Tulare County is strategically located along Highway 99 and near California’s major ports and rail corridors. Our central access, affordable land, and available sites make the region an ideal fit for commercial, retail, industrial, manufacturing, and logistics investment.

What sets us apart?

Fast Permitting: Among the fastest permitting timelines in California

No Impact Fees: Reduced development costs and fewer barriers to entry

Pro-Business Climate: Local, state, and federal incentive guidance

Workforce Development: Deep partnerships with training and education providers

Industry Diversity: From agriculture and manufacturing to tourism and clean energy

While our office leads development efforts for unincorporated areas, we proudly collaborate with all eight incorporated cities in Tulare County to support regional prosperity and shared economic goals.

Whether you’re launching your next expansion, opening a new facility, or simply looking for a community ready to grow with you—GROW Tulare County is here to help.

Website: www.growtularecounty.org
Email: economicdevelopment@tularecounty.ca.gov
Phone: (559) 624-7177

Name: PANACEA - H2020 Project
Description:

Name: Warren-Washington Industrial Development Agency
Description:
The WWIDA provides incentive-based economic development programs designed to help businesses expand or relocate within Warren and Washington counties in New York. We partner with local businesses, developers, and municipalities to deliver strategic incentives and financing programs that support job creation, capital investment, and community growth. 


Our investment incentives include property, sales, and mortgage tax exemptions, as well as tax-exempt bond financing. In addition, the WWIDA owns and manages two industrial parks, offering parcels of varying sizes available for sale or lease to accommodate a wide range of business needs. 

Whether you’re a local entrepreneur or an established company seeking expansion opportunities in Upstate New York, WWIDA is your trusted partner for promoting local investment.

Created by the voters of Jacksonville in 1995, JEDCO is a Type B economic development corporation. The first chair of the board was Senator Robert L. Nichols. JEDCO is led by a seven-member Board of Directors appointed by the Jacksonville City Council.

Name: NorCal AngelCon
Description:
Angel Investors propel early-stage startups with much-needed seed capital to cover initial expenses such as product development, market research, and early operations. Their willingness to invest early and take on risk that institutional lenders often won’t help entrepreneurs who may not have a proven track record.

Beyond providing funding, many angel investors also offer mentorship, guidance, and connections to help startups navigate challenges, make strategic decisions, and avoid common pitfalls. This hands-on involvement can be rewarding for the Investor and can significantly increase the chances of success for the startup.

To support the development of more engaged capital networks in the Greater Sacramento Region, the California Office of the Small Business Advocate (CalOSBA) has funded the launch of NorCal AngelCon, an investment education program designed to engage new angel investors in learning how to source, screen, diligence, and invest in pre-seed / seed stage venture backable startups. 

This program culminates in a startup showcase at which the cohort of Angels will announce their investment into one of the regional startups they diligenced during the program.

The CTIDA seeks to promote a vibrant, diversified, and sustainable economy to enhance the quality of life in College Township and throughout the Centre Region and Centre County. The CTIDA will actively support, encourage, and further the interests of new and existing businesses and industry for the purposes of retaining and expanding existing job opportunities, attracting new jobs, and stimulating the investment of capital into College Township.

In today's economic climate, it can be difficult for small business owners to acquire the loans needed to grow their established businesses or for entrepreneurs to acquire the funds needed to start a new venture. Frequently, borrower's collateral is insufficient to justify a bank lending a full requested amount. At the CTIDA, we know and have developed programs to help! We offer Loan Guarantor Program, Co-Lending Program, Direct Lending Options, and Grants.

Name: Neeganii Iishawin
Description:
Neeganii Iishawin, 'to lead to the Gathering place,' has become a symbol of Indigenous economic empowerment and collaboration. Representing 34 communities' with five Tribal Council partners, Neeganii Iishawin began as a collective resolve to drive economic growth through strategic partnerships, and is now developing into a Northern Network to facilitate the exchange of Indigenous capacity-building insights, education and training.  

The six partner organizations are Windigo Community Development Corporation, Matawa First Nations, Keewaytinook Okimakanak, Shibogama First Nations Council, TKG Group and Independent First Nations Alliance.

Name: EcoMod, Global Economic Modeling Network
Description:
EcoMod is the world’s leading research, advisory, and educational network dedicated to promoting advanced modeling and statistical techniques in economic policy and decision making. EcoMod brings together thousands of modeling experts and users worldwide.

EcoMod fellows have unparalleled expertise in developing and using models in impact assessment, scenario analysis, forecasting, projections, strategic planning, and policy development.

EcoMod fellows have developed projects in scenario and impact analysis in the fields of international trade, development, environment, energy, public finance, tax policy, monetary policy, agriculture, labor market issues, forecasting and projections in European, Asian, Latin American, Middle East and North African countries, and in the USA.

EcoMod’s research, training and workshops focus on policy-relevant issues in order to help decision-making and to bridge the gap between academics, policymakers, and business.

EcoMod Modeling School is the largest provider of intensive economic modeling courses in the world through its regular training programs in Europe, in the USA, and in Asia, and through its on-line and on-site courses.

EcoMod provides consulting and training services to a large number of institutions in more than 100 countries including all the major international organizations, many governments, central banks, and several of the world’s largest companies.

EcoMod’s conferences and workshops bring together hundreds of participants from all continents active in the policy modeling field.

Name: East Coast Connected
Description:
ECC is re-launching in 2025 with a focus  on connecting Atlantic Canadians from all corners of the world who want to build great companies. More to come...
________________________________




What is East Coast Connected (ECC)?

Major economic centres like Toronto and Ottawa are home to thousands of “ex-pat” Atlantic Canadians. These people have left the East Coast to advance their careers, to pursue secondary education opportunities, to honour family commitments, or to encounter different cultural experiences than they can find in their home provinces. They never lose their love for the Atlantic region. 

East Coast Connected believes that Atlantic Canadians who have moved away are still, at heart, Atlantic Canadians. More than that, we believe that they are still a valuable resource for their home provinces – whether you’re talking about investment opportunities or fundraising for your former local charity, this diverse group of people is full of passion and goodwill. 

Since 2007, East Coast Connected has been a forum for discussion, dialogue, networking, and informal mentoring. We aim to take advantage of the momentum generated since our inception. How? By channelling the energy of our members into doing good work, and by reaffirming our role as a key professional networking hub for Atlantic Canadians.

Our team of experienced economic development professionals and municipal strategists partner with cities, counties, and special districts to identify revenue opportunities, attract investment, and build the infrastructure that powers communities. We take a data-driven, solutions-oriented approach, ensuring that every project, whether it's business attraction, infrastructure development, or strategic planning delivers measurable impact.

- Public Infrastructure Financing Solutions

Name: WEECAP - Women's Economic Empowerment through Cashew Processing

Il opère en Côte d'Ivoire, en Guinée-Bissau et au Sénégal afin de créer une économie de la noix de cajou plus compétitive et plus inclusive.
Ensemble, ces partenaires poursuivent un objectif clair et ambitieux : créer 330 000 emplois décents et durables pour les jeunes femmes et les jeunes hommes en Côte d’Ivoire, en Guinée-Bissau et au Sénégal.

WEECAP considère l’emploi non pas comme une simple conséquence de l’activité économique, mais comme un résultat stratégique, découlant de la compétitivité et de la performance durable des systèmes de production et de transformation du cajou.

Faire du cajou un moteur d’emplois et de croissance industrielle en Afrique de l'Ouest

Name: Indonesia Economic Summit 2026
Description:
The Indonesia Economic Summit (IES) is an annual initiative of the Indonesia Business Council (IBC). Initiated as a high-level forum for collaboration and innovation, IES seeks to promote competitiveness, inclusive growth, and sustainable prosperity for Indonesia. This forum reflects IBC's commitment to advancing Indonesia's role in the global economic arena.

IES 2025 held  in Shangri-La, Jakarta, attended by more than 1,500 participants from 48 countries. The two-day summit (Feb 18-19), offers a forum for 100 speakers representing policy makers, business leaders, experts, and academics.

Name: IMBE
Description:
In South Africa, 1.3 million children aged 3 to 5 lack access to early learning, with the highest disparities in under-resourced communities. Unlike other education sectors, ECD services are run by small, owner-operated businesses relying on parental fees, making them financially vulnerable.

At IMBE, we support women-led ECD microenterprises with innovative business solutions to enhance financial resilience. While passionate about childcare, many lack business skills and financial access to sustain their centres. Our programmes equip them with tools to grow their income, expanding access to quality early learning.

By supporting IMBE, funders contribute to a sustainable solution that empowers entrepreneurs, strengthens businesses, and increases ECD access—especially for disadvantaged children.
