Name: StartupCourt
Description:
Startupcourt empowers Black and immigrant founders to build ventures that create lasting social and economic impact. Our mission is to break down barriers, unlock opportunities, and build sustainable solutions that uplift underrepresented communities.

We know that underrepresented entrepreneurs face unique challenges — limited access to capital, professional networks, and business resources. That’s why we go beyond traditional support models, offering a comprehensive ecosystem of mentorship, funding pathways, and operational expertise to help founders launch, grow, and scale successful businesses.

Name: National Indigenous Economic Development Board
Description: Le Conseil est composé de dirigeants des affaires et des collectivités des Premières nations, des Inuits et des Métis de tout le Canada. Ensemble, nous conseillons le gouvernement fédéral sur les moyens d’accroître la participation  des peuples autochtones à l’économie canadienne.

Name: Campusatsea
Description: Onder de vlag van Campus@Sea komen partijen samen die innoveren op de Noordzee en aan de kust. Het is niet alleen een fysieke plek in Den Haag; bovenal is het een netwerk van netwerken dat duurzame innovatie aanjaagt. Op de vier hoofdthema’s voedsel, (nieuwe) energie, ecologie en sport worden co-creatie projecten en evenementen ondernomen om te testen en leren. Vanuit Campus@Sea wordt er gezorgd voor de broodnodige ruimte voor praktijkexperimenten en ondersteuning voor co-creatie en ondernemers. Waardevolle en noodzakelijke elementen om innovatie van de grond te krijgen en uiteindelijk op te schalen voor breed gebruik. Zo fungeert Campus@Sea als landelijke hotspot, waar vanuit hartje Scheveningen, innovatie en economische kennis gelinkt aan de Noordzee samen komen. Zo wordt er gewerkt aan een positieve impact op de wereld van morgen.

Name: Collaborative EDO
Description:
Collaborative Economic Development Oregon focuses on strategic engagement in activities that drive economic activity, such as business development, workforce development, business recruitment and innovation. Our organization works with public and private partners to identify and support opportunities for growth, and leverages our connections to attract investment to the region. Through collaboration and partnership, Collaborative EDO is working to position Lane County as a hub of economic activity in the Willamette Valley in Oregon.

Collaborative EDO exemplifies what happens when people who are passionate about our community come together to make a collective impact with a vision for a larger geographic impact.

Collaborative EDO is headquartered in Lane County and is working in collaboration with other economic development organizations and stakeholders across Oregon. We invite anyone interested in economic development in Lane County to join us in our mission to drive economic growth and prosperity in the region.

Name: Urbanics
Description: חברת אורבניקס מובילה בתחום הייעוץ התכנוני, הכלכלי והסביבתי. החברה הינה רב תחומית החורטת על דגלה מקצועיות, חדשנות ומתן שירות אמין  ללקוחותיה. באורבניקס אנחנו יודעים להיות דינמיים ולהתאים עצמנו למציאות המשתנה. אורבניקס הינה בעלת תרומה רבה לקביעת המדיניות ברמה הארצית . והמקומית

Name: EOG - Gemeente Almere (Economische Ontwikkeling & Grondzaken)
Description: Deze LinkedIn pagina is voor ondernemend Almere en wordt onderhouden door team EOG. Wij delen hier zoveel mogelijk informatie, onze successen en resultaten. Dit doen we met elkaar en voor elkaar. Actuele updates betreft economische zaken komen aan de orde, we houden jullie graag op de hoogte.

Name: First Nations Tax Commission (FNTC)
Description: Established in 2005 following the passage of the First Nations Fiscal Management Act (FMA), the First Nations Tax Commission (FNTC) regulates, supports and advances property taxation on reserve lands. The FNTC supports the FMA framework through the development and implementation of First Nation taxation systems, expanding jurisdictions, fostering economic development, and promoting greater self-determination. Through these efforts, the FNTC contributes to creating the legal infrastructure necessary to enable First Nations to participate in the regional market economy and create a competitive investment climate on their lands.

Name: Economic Modelling Academy
Description:
EMA is your gateway to the world of economic modeling

EMA partners with ADRS (Applied Development Research Solutions), a world leader in the field of economic modelling. Over the last 20 years, ADRS has built a system of advanced analytical intelligence composed of economic models for more than 65 countries.

As an EMA student, you will have access to the wealth of resource, experience, expertise and more than sixty country models developed by ADRS. Click on the tiles in the graphic below to discover more about economic models that you will learn about and gain access to through EMA courses.

Name: Fort McMurray 468 First Nation Economic Development
Description: The Economic Development Corporation is a stand-alone entity under the Fort McMurray 468 First Nation. We exist to support the economic success of the Nation and create long-term benefits for its Members. We do this by developing and managing business and equity opportunities for the Nation and Fort McMurray 468 First Nation Group of Companies through a focus on building meaningful business partnerships and industry relationships.

Name: 360 Degree Society
Description:
The places where we spend our time matter and great places are made up of people working together to achieve shared aims. We’re passionate about making a difference to communities through the work that we do and the way we live our lives.

The 360 Degree Society is a community of experts and changemakers who share the same values, many of us having spent our entire careers striving to create better places and outcomes for the people in our communities. We believe that creating a healthier, more sustainable and vibrant society is possible and can be achieved if we pull together and work differently. 

Our shared mission is to make the world a greater place through supporting people, teams, organisations, systems and places to affect the life outcomes of those that live within our communities and in wider society.

Name: The Jackson County Development Authority
Description:
WHAT WE DO:
Neighborhood Development Strategies
Business Start-Up & Entrepreneur Support
Workforce Development
Strategic Planning
Marketing & Attraction
Finance Assistance & Incentivizing
Real Estate Development & Reuse
Credit Analysis
Business Retention & Expansion
Professional business incubation rentals at a low cost
Commercial, Industrial, & Residential land database
Data & Statistics

The Jackson County Development Authority offers technical assistance at no charge to businesses both small and large. The Jackson County Development Authority partners with organizations to provide funding for flexible, customized training programs that offer assistance to businesses and directly supports the transfer of knowledge and skills. The Jackson County Development Authority wants your business to succeed. In partnership with local, state and national organizations the JCDA can assist you in acquiring the knowledge and tools to plan, launch, manage, and grow your Jackson County business.

Name: Lansing Economic Development Corporation
Description: The Lansing EDC is a nonprofit economic development organization focused exclusively on the city of Lansing, Michigan.   Our mission is to improve the Lansing community by fostering economic growth that is strategic, sustainable and equitable.  The Lansing EDC does this by helping businesses, redeveloping properties and addressing economic inequalities.

We foster new opportunities for investors and stakeholders by building strong alliances and developing supportive ecosystems. Our mission is to empower Small Medium Enterprises, boost economic growth, create more jobs across Saudi Arabia.

Name: Golf Tourism Solutions

We believe there’s magic in a Myrtle Beach golf experience. 

We collectively promote, elevate, and advance The Golf Capital of the World. 

Our efforts are designed around five interconnected areas of focus:

Marketing
Events 
Innovation
Research
Membership

While each has its own distinct purpose, no single area will advance our work alone.
Location: Greater Myrtle Beach Area, South Carolina

Name: Straumr
Description: Straumr tilbyr en plattform for forståelse, benchmarking og simulering av inntektsrammer. Vi har utviklet et analyseverktøy som både visualiserer nøkkeldata fra de siste publiserte inntektsrammene, åpner for benchmarking av sentrale KPIer og gir mulighet til å sette opp, simulere og sammenligne hvordan investeringer i infrastruktur påvirker selskapets inntektsramme, effektivitet og avkastning på kapital.

Name: La Plata Economic Development Alliance
Description: Welcome to the La Plata Economic Development Alliance. Whether you are thinking about creating, expanding, relocating your business, the Alliance and its partners welcome you and want to provide you with full community resources, information and services to make your business endeavors in our County a success. Our mission is to create a vibrant business community and a range of employment opportunities for our citizens through retention, expansion, and recruitment of quality businesses.

Name: Task Force Movement
Description: Bringing Transitioning Service Members, Veterans, Military Families, and industry stakeholders together to improve economic and national security outcomes.

Name: Expo Mauritania إكسبو موريتانيا

معرض "أكسبو القطاع الخاص موريتانيا 2025" هو منصة للتبادل والابتكار وفرص الأعمال في موريتانيا.

Name: Calhoun County Economic Development Council
Description:
Calhoun County Economic Development Council assists existing and prospective industries in Calhoun County, AL.

We help businesses improve business retention and growth to increase employment opportunities. Our organization serves to improve the quality of life and promote the county as a desirable place to live, learn, and work. Learn more about training programs, financial and tax incentives, infrastructure, and government rules and regulations by visiting our website.

Name: HEC Economic Advisory
Description:
L’association de macroéconomie et de géopolitique d’HEC Paris qui a plusieurs missions :  

- Rédiger des notes de synthèse pour nos partenaires

- Préparer les étudiants d’HEC Paris aux métiers de l'économie au sens large (Secteur Public, Sovereign Advisory, Banque ...) via la rencontre de professionnels, et l'entraînement aux process. 

- Organisation de conférences d'acteurs majeurs du monde économique

Name: Gestion du Capital
Description: Gestion du Capital is a financial-education platform offering hands-on training in portfolio management, trading, crypto, commodities and more. Tailored to both beginners and seasoned investors, our courses blend real-world case studies, advanced tools and personalised guidance to help you build a strong financial future.

Name: People's Economy Lab
Description:
Imagine an economy that serves people, instead of people serving the economy. Where community and worker-owned businesses meet local needs. Where the government is driven by participation instead of partisan politics. Where investing means closing the racial wealth gap instead of making the rich richer.

People’s Economy Lab identifies, co-creates, and makes the case for better economic models. We convene leaders, develop and test better models, and push the bounds of what’s possible for a Just Transition to a solidarity economy.

Name: Greater Cleveland Works
Description: Greater Cleveland Works is leading the way to a better workforce system for all. As the workforce development board for Cleveland and Cuyahoga County, we deliver impactful services to people and employers, raise and distribute funding to strengthen the system, and collaborate with partners to improve job quality, reduce racial disparities, and grow the regional economy.

Name: ÉcoSpark | Économy Spark
Description: Inspirer, informer et illuminer votre chemin en économie, business et innovation

Name: Circulair Utrecht
Description: Wil jij jouw bedrijf toekomstbestendig maken met circulaire oplossingen? Sluit je aan bij Circulair Utrecht: de vereniging voor én door ondernemers in de regio Utrecht. Samen werken we aan de transitie naar een circulaire economie, leren we van elkaar en creëren we commerciële circulaire kansen.

Name: Focus North
Description: Focus North will deliver transformational projects to ensure the North of mainland Scotland generates growth, drives economic diversity, and leads the low carbon economy.

Name: Wirtschaftsjunioren Hannover
Description:
Herzlich willkommen bei uns. 

Die Wirtschaftsjunioren Hannover sind ein Netzwerk aus jungen Unternehmern und Führungskräften unter 40 in Hannover.

Wir veranstalten Netzwerkveranstaltungen, Unternehmensbesichtigungen und viele weitere Formate.

Wenn ihr euch angesprochen fühlt, schaut gerne bei uns vorbei.

Name: Watt Watchers fr
Description:
Watt Watchers est un programme CEE soutenu par le gouvernement français et conçu pour aider les foyers à réduire leur consommation d’énergie et adopter des pratiques plus sobres. 

Il vise à accompagner 600 000 ménages d'ici 2027 en leur proposant un parcours personnalisé, comprenant des conseils sur les écogestes, des équipements économes, et un accompagnement pour la rénovation énergétique.

Le programme a deux enjeux : 
- l'accompagnement à la sobriété énergétique pour tous via un axe digital (application et contenus digitaux) et un axe physique (sensibilisation sur le terrain).
- Mesurer l'impact réel des économies d'énergie grâce aux données des compteurs communicants

Vous pouvez vous connecter à l'application Watt Watchers gratuitement en quelques clicks : https://www.winter-energies.fr/app/inscription

Name: The Middle Georgia Innovation Corridor
Description: The Middle Georgia Innovation Corridor is a vibrant center for technological, scientific, and digital innovation comprised of 11 counties in the region. Positioned at the crossroads of key transportation routes and adjacent to Robins Air Force Base, its strategic location fosters seamless connection, commerce, and collaboration. Middle Georgia’s strategic location, high quality of life, affordable living, world-class educational institutions, and pioneering businesses and high-tech partnerships make the region the ideal location for innovation, careers, and families to thrive. Learn more at www.MiddleGeorgiaInnovates.com.

Name: Intelligence Economic International
Description:
Programul postuniversitar INT-E produce valoare adăugata în două etape cruciale: prin transformarea informațiilor în cunoaștere și a cunoașterii în dezvoltare sustenabilă.  
Intelligence-ul economic (IE) reprezintă informația procesată și administrată într-un mod deștept ce permite posesorului să înțeleagă și să anticipeze mediul exterior. 
Evaluarea acestui mediu reprezentat de: competitorii cheie, regulile și efectele lor, trend-uri și mize etc., este necesară în indepărtarea erorilor și în prevenirea riscurilor de gândire și funcționare a unei afaceri. 
De la alocarea de resurse și modul în care acestea se pot completa intern, până la modul în care dezvoltarea pe piețe externe este articulată.

Name: Granbury Economic Development
Description: The Mission of the City of Granbury Economic Development Department is to proactively recruit economic development opportunities and new businesses to the City of Granbury and Hood County, work with existing businesses to assist with Business Retention & Expansion, support development of public infrastructure, and serve as an information clearinghouse for the City, County and the area business community.

Name: FEP Economics Society
Description: Somos alunos de Mestrado e Licenciatura empenhados em criar uma organização de excelência, cujo principal objetivo é o de levar o pensamento, debate e estudo económico para o panorama associativo da faculdade. Segue-nos no instagram para mais novidades: @fepeconomicssociety

Name: UBC VMS
Description:
The UBC Venture Mentoring Service (VMS) is a confidential, team-based mentorship program that connects innovators with a team of experienced volunteer mentors. Grounded in the internationally recognized VMS mentorship model developed by MIT, UBC VMS provides structured, supportive and secure guidance to strengthen leadership, improve entrepreneurial decision-making and support the translation of innovation into impact. We understand each venture is inherently unique and tailor our mentoring services to mentees’ individual needs and schedules.  

Free of charge and open to UBC students, alumni, faculty, staff and research partners, UBC VMS is delivered through UBC Okanagan’s Office of the Vice-Principal, Research and Innovation.  Join us as we create a vibrant community of innovators and leaders in the Okanagan. 

**If you're looking for entrepreneurship@UBCO, this programming is now delivered across both UBC campuses through Innovation UBC.  Visit and follow for support and programs that foster entrepreneurial skills development and venture-building capacity among UBC researchers. https://www.linkedin.com/company/innovationubc

Name: Alabama Council on Economic Education - ACEE
Description:
The major purpose of the Alabama Council on Economic Education is to promote a better understanding of the American economy and its relationship to international, national, state and local economic issues. The activities and programs of ACEE are to be directed toward objective, comprehensive, and balanced economic and educational programs.
 
In the promotion of economic understanding and financial literacy, ACEE is to serve as a resource for schools and teacher training throughout Alabama as well as an advocate for economic education and financial literacy.  Through the development of partnerships among all educational institutions and systems, professional organizations of teachers and school administrators, ACEE has the purpose of promoting greater understanding of the principles, practices and values of America’s free enterprise heritage and economic system through teacher education.

Name: LAC Investment Network
Port Of Lewiston
Description: Port of Lewiston is a multi-modal transportation hub that creates opportunities for jobs and trade while enhancing the quality of life for our residents.

Name: Mount Pleasant Made
Description:
Rocket Fuel

Mount Pleasant is the launchpad for what’s next.

From breakthrough tech to game-changing life science, companies here don’t just grow — they take off. With unmatched talent, global connectivity, and a community built for momentum, Mount Pleasant is where bold ideas become world-class realities.

Name: Wirtschaftsförderung und Regionalmanagement Waldeck-Frankenberg GmbH
Description:
"Wir fördern unternehmerisches Engagement und setzen uns dafür ein, die Wirtschaftskraft von Waldeck-Frankenberg weiter zu stärken."
Dieses erreichen wir, indem wir in engem Austausch mit den öffentlichen wie privaten Akteuren stehen. Unternehmen und Gründer*Innen unterstützen wir bestmöglich bei ihren Vorhaben. Projekte treiben wir voran. Wir steigern die Bekanntheit der Region nach außen, pflegen und erweitern unser Netzwerk, um diesen starken und innovativen Wirtschaftsstandort weiter auszubauen.

Name: etcétera | centro de estudios
Description:
Somos Centro de Estudios y divulgación sin fines de lucro que nuclea a especialistas en diversos campos de las ciencias económicas para contribuir al debate ciudadano de calidad para el desarrollo de Uruguay.

Combinamos rigurosidad técnica con un abordaje pedagógico para permitir la comprensión de la economía por parte del público no especializado. Estamos involucrados en una amplia gama de proyectos vinculados con seguimiento de coyuntura económica, estudios sectoriales o locales, análisis de política económica, monitoreo de opinión pública o divulgación de tópicos y debates económicos. Nuestros productos se materializan en informes públicos y privados y actividades públicas, así como en columnas de divulgación en prensa, radio y televisión.

La calidad y amplitud en la producción de conocimiento por parte de etcétera se apoya en una red horizontal y plural especializada en temas económicos llamada Grupo Jueves. Esta red contribuye mediante la revisión entre pares a mejorar los productos generados por etcétera.

Name: Regional Economic Studies Institute (RESI)
Description: The Regional Economic Studies Institute has 35 years of experience providing a vast array of economic and policy analysis services. Our solutions empower public, private and nonprofit organizational decision-making throughout Maryland and across the region. As economic factors and public policy are ever-evolving, RESI works to boost and expand services and expertise to meet the needs of our partners.

Name: FAI NRW
Description:
As part of the Fachkräfte-Offensive NRW, we offer free, practice-oriented support tailored specifically to the needs of companies in North Rhine-Westphalia. 

The FAI NRW team supports companies every step of the way - from needs analysis to recruitment and integration. We offer free support and provide practical advice on how to overcome legal and bureaucratic challenges. With the help of structured checklists, we make it easier to manage immigration-related documents. We also advise companies in all phases of integrating new employees and support them in setting up and maintaining welcome structures that promote long-term cooperation. We pay particular attention to family reunification, where we are also on hand to advise you. Our comprehensive services range from practical workshops, information events and helpful publications to checklists and best-practice approaches through to individual advice before and after recruitment. 

The project is supported by the Ministry of Labor, Health and Social Affairs and funded by the European Union.

Name: Wellbeing Economy Cymru (WE Cymru)
Description:

Calling for solidarity in Wales for economic system change.

Mae canolbwynt / A hub of Wellbeing Economy Alliance - WEAll.

Name: Knox County Indiana Economic Development
Description: Knox County Indiana Economic Development (KCIED) leads, attracts, and catalyzes economic growth through collaborations, programs, and strategies.   KCIED promotes a strong business climate through proactive working partnerships with government, business, entrepreneurs, education, non-profits, and the community at large.

Name: Greater Roanoke & NRV SBDC
Description:
Confidential business advising. Engaging programs and learning opportunities. Free resources and research. No strings attached. 

We drive economic development in our region through small business growth in every industry.

Name: Fruitpact
Description:
Het Fruitpact is een bijzonder samenwerkingsverband tussen overheid, ondernemers en kennisorganisaties in Fruitdelta Rivierenland: de Betuwe, het Land van Maas en Waal en de Bommelerwaard. De fruitteelt is van oudsher sterk op deze plek verankerd en heeft veel aanverwante bedrijvigheid, onderwijs en toerisme aangetrokken. Daarmee heeft de sector zich nationaal en regionaal ontwikkeld tot een economisch fruitcluster van betekenis. 
In het licht van (inter)nationale economische ontwikkelingen wil het Fruitpact de Nederlandse en internationale concurrentiepositie van het fruitcluster versterken en uitbouwen. Met haalbare doelstellingen en concrete projecten, die op korte en lange termijn tastbaar resultaat opleveren. Voor grote en kleine bedrijven, voor werkgevers, werknemers en inwoners.

Name: Communities Economic Development Fund Manitoba
Description:
The Communities Economic Development Fund (CEDF) is a Manitoba Crown Corporation that has operated in northern Manitoba since 1973 with the mission to build communities and create connections and business development. 

We provide community members with an information and resource centre, business development support, access to capital, and community building and engagement services. In addition, we host communications and marketing events to support those who are looking to start or improve their entrepreneurial career. 

With the right people with the right attitude, we’re leading this movement to ensure northern Manitoba realizes its economic potential.

Name: Scaling Change Inc.
Description:
* Equipping the Next Generation of Social Entrepreneurs *
We believe in people like you. Because people like you move us forward. You are crazy enough to think you can change the world. And it's people like you who actually do.  

Join us.

Name: Polskie Towarzystwo Ekonomiczne w Katowicach
Description: Organizacja konferencji międzynarodowych i krajowych. Doradztwo, szkolenia, ekspertyzy i debaty z zakresu ekonomii, finansów i zarządzania.

Name: Ecuador Circular - Economía Circular
Description:
Este espacio de networking está pensado para que funcione como un directorio de actores, iniciativas, negocios que convergen en un solo espacio e interactúan entre sí para generar conversación, compartir y ahondar en intereses en común, generar contactos y sinergias en torno a la temática de economía circular.  

Este espacio nace al momento de desarrollar el primer ecosistema digital "Ecuador Circular" - Plataforma Nacional de Economía Circular. Bajo este concepto, se llevó a cabo un taller en el cual se vio la necesidad de idear el desarrollo de un espacio para fomentar el networking circular mediante la creación de un directorio dinámico de contactos, redes, eventos y consultas relacionadas con EC en el Ecuador. 

Te invitamos a navegar por la principal plataforma digital para la transición del Ecuador hacia una Economía Circular. Participa, junto a otros actores, en la generación de información actualizada, noticias de interés, eventos, y espacios de articulación y oportunidades de colaboración.

Name: The Economic Development Association for Black Communities
Description:
EDABC champions inclusive economic growth by advancing Black-led innovation, entrepreneurship, and workforce development in Colorado. We serve as a bridge between communities, government agencies, and corporate partners committed to equity and sustainability.

With a focus on data-informed strategies, place-based investment, and policy advocacy, EDABC strengthens ecosystems where Black communities thrive. From small business capacity building to regional economic planning, we work collaboratively to ensure economic development is equitable, scalable, and rooted in lived experience.

Join us in reshaping systems, unlocking capital, and amplifying Black economic agency.

Name: After h’all
Description:
L’After h’all incarne les valeurs de solidarité, d’audace, et de faire-ensemble, ancrées dans l’ADN de la CPME du Rhône. 

Véritable lieu d’expérimentation, il valorise toutes les initiatives vertueuses, qu’elles soient entrepreneuriales ou citoyennes. 

Accélérateur d’idées, il favorise l’apprentissage par la mutualisation des connaissances et des bonnes pratiques.

Name: KCnomics, LLC
Description: KCnomics, LLC is a privately-owned economic forecasting, real estate advisory, valuation, and expert witness research firm serving the commercial real estate industry nation-wide providing insights and answers to complex questions that impact real estate investments and transactions. Areas of specialty include economic feasibility analysis, appraisal and property tax appeal, bank real estate regulatory compliance, CRE Finance, ports, logistics and supply-chain, affordable housing, land rezoning, adaptive reuse, and “Big-Box” retail.

Name: BRICS Türkiye
Description: BRICS, Brezilya, Rusya, Hindistan, Çin ve Güney Afrika'nın İngilizce baş harflerinden oluşan, daha sonra birliğe üye olan Mısır, İran, Etiyopya ve Birleşik Arap Emirlikleri ile 9 daimi üyeye ulaşan hükümetler arası ekonomik bir örgüttür.

Name: Bergische Struktur- und Wirtschaftsförderungsgesellschaft mbH
Description:
Die Bergische Struktur- und Wirtschaftsförderungsgesellschaft mbH initiiert und organisiert regionale Projekte sowie die Zusammenarbeit im Bergischen Städtedreieck – Remscheid, Solingen und Wuppertal. Die  Themenfelder Wirtschaftsförderung, Strukturentwicklung und Tourismus werden von der Bergischen Gesellschaft im regionalen Kontext und in Abstimmung mit der Landesstrategie umgesetzt. 

In unserem Newsletter monatlichen Newsletter berichten wir über Projekte, Menschen und Entwicklungen im Bergischen Land – regional verwurzelt, international vernetzt.
Dazu: Einblicke, Empfehlungen, Ausflugstipps.
Hier abonnieren: https://bergische-gesellschaft.de/newsletter/

Name: EXA Group Canada
Description:
EXA Group Canada and the Strong Farm & Rural Shores Destinations initiative are dedicated to empowering rural, remote, and waterside communities through transformative tourism and innovative agriculture. We design and deliver strategies that strengthen local economies, foster regenerative practices, and create opportunities for growth that respect culture, heritage, and the environment.

Our work bridges tourism, agriculture, and economic development, enabling communities to adapt to global challenges while building resilience for the future. Through collaboration with governments, industry leaders, non-profits, and academic institutions, we develop projects that drive innovation, increase capacity, and connect local strengths to global markets.

From destination development to climate-smart agriculture, from workforce training to investment partnerships, we are committed to unlocking the potential of rural and remote regions creating vibrant, sustainable places where people and nature thrive together.

Stay tuned for more details soon.

Name: EXA Group Canada
Description:
We are ushering in a new era of development, with a strong focus on sustainable agriculture, regenerative tourism, rural growth, technology, innovation, and economic advancement.

Name: Journal of Tropical Futures
Description:
The Journal of Tropical Futures: Sustainable Business, Governance & Development (JTF) is a peer-reviewed journal from SAGE Publications administered by James Cook University, Singapore. JTF is a multidisciplinary journal that promotes sustainable and responsible business, management and governance in the tropics.

Situated between the Tropics of Cancer and Capricorn, the countries that make up the tropics are largely developing in nature and some of the fastest growing regions in the world. JTF seeks to represent and address the complex and heterogeneous nature of the challenges facing these regions by exploring the interrelationship between business, management, political economy, development and the environment. We are particularly interested in the many tensions that exist between demands for economic growth, social and material wellbeing of populations and corresponding environmental impacts. Can the developmental needs of peoples be met in equitable ways by the expansion of business, trade and innovation in the tropics? What forms of responsible stewardship, organisational practice, resource management and governance might help navigate the unique and precarious concerns of the tropics?

Name: The Chisholm Group, LLC
Description:
The Chisholm Group is an economic justice organization committed to real impact and systemic transformation through educational & workforce opportunity, strategic partnerships, and entrepreneurship.

The mission of the Chisholm Group, LLC is to transform generational trajectories by connecting education and economic pathways in cities across America, bringing these sectors together to create stronger pathways to the future. Our approach is based on a model of targeted universalism. This means setting ambitious goals to prepare all students for the future but beginning with those historically locked out of the opportunity.

The Chisholm Group currently provides education agency, government, non-profit, and business consulting services at the national, state, and local level to address systemic barriers in access to economic opportunity for marginalized communities. 

The Chisholm Group also coaches Black women, women of color, and non-binary individuals on how to begin their own entrepreneurial journey to launch or expand a business venture, breaking down barriers to economic opportunity.

The Chisholm Group is named after the late Congresswoman Shirley Chisholm, the first Black woman to run for President of the United States from a major party. Ms. Chisholm was an education, economic justice, and women’s rights warrior. The Chisholm Group pays homage to our ancestors by continuing their legacy of lifting as we climb. The Chisholm Group will always be driven to be “unbought and unbossed”, and a “catalyst for change”.

Name: Consejo Andaluz de Colegios de Gestores Administrativos

La entidad se conforma como órgano de representación de la profesión a nivel andaluz y forma parte de la configuración a nivel estatal del Consejo General de Colegios de Gestores Administrativos.

La entidad engloba aproximadamente a 740 colegiados de Andalucía que obligatoriamente deben pertenecer a la misma. En concreto, el Consejo Andaluz representa a 278 gestores administrativos del Colegio de Granada, Jaén y Almería; 160 de Málaga; y 312 del Colegio de Sevilla.

Los Gestores Administrativos se definen como “aquellos que, de modo habitual y por profesión se dedican libremente a promover y activar en las oficinas públicas, mediante la percepción de honorarios, toda clase de asuntos de particulares o corporaciones”. Por su especialización pueden actuar ante cualquier Administración Pública en nombre de las personas físicas o jurídicas, sin más limitación que las reservadas expresamente a otras profesiones y con la garantía de un comportamiento adecuado a unas normas expresas y una responsabilidad civil obligatoria. 

Entre las especializaciones de los Gestores Administrativos se encuentran la asesoría laboral, fiscal y contable; la gestión de ayudas y subvenciones; las tramitaciones relativas al tráfico y los transportes; las herencias; la creación de empresas; los trámites de extranjería; los registros; las patetentes y marcas y la gestión de escrituras.

Name: energonomy
Description: The political economy of energy has become an increasingly important area of study in recent years. Energy is a critical input to all economic activities, and the cost, availability, and sustainability of energy have a significant impact on economic development and growth, social welfare, and the environment. This startup seeks to provide an overview of the political economy of energy, with a particular focus on the interactions between government regulation, market forces, and technological innovation. We begin by discussing the historical evolution of the political economy of energy, tracing the development of energy markets and government regulation from the early days of oil exploration to the present day. We then examine the key theoretical approaches to the study of the political economy of energy, including neoclassical economics, political science, and ecological economics. Finally, we explore some of the key policy challenges facing policymakers in the energy sector, including the need to balance economic growth with environmental sustainability, the challenge of managing energy transitions in a rapidly changing global economy, and the importance of promoting energy security and international cooperation.

Name: Team Spirit International
Description:
Team Spirit International (TSI) is a mission-driven organization working together with refugee leaders and communities in Uganda to enhance their self-reliance, leverage their capacities and co-create more livelihood opportunities. TSI aims to strengthen enabling impact ecosystems through synthesized, innovative training and incubation programs, enhanced cross-sectoral collaboration, grassroot leadership and targeted resource provision. Our vision is to create a world where refugees are recognized as leaders of tomorrow and have platforms of opportunity to lead thriving lives with impact and self-determination.

Previous Milestones
TSI has surveyed over 400 individuals and conducted two years of research, implementing two training programs that directly impacted 100 people and indirectly reached over 400. In 2023, TSI launched the "Agroecology and Business Bootcamp," training 59 farmers, distributing over 200 tools, and forming six farmer collectives. This led to a 13% income increase and 11% yield growth. TSI has participated in two Harvard incubators (Harvard i-labs and LPCE), built its own startup infrastructure, and raised $20,000 through crowdfunding. Key partners include Unleashed, Relief Aid Foundation (founded by bootcamp participants), Tomorrow Vijana, and 16 community-based organizations, as well as the African Leadership University.

High-Impact Incubator
In July 2024, TSI and Unleashed launched the High-Impact Incubator, uniting refugee, community, and student leaders to co-create a new leadership paradigm focused on personal development, locally led transformation, and high-impact entrepreneurship. The first phase trained 18 organizations from 11 Ugandan settlements, forming the "Leaders of Today" consortium. The second phase runs until October, where 22 leaders will mentor 320 entrepreneurs. In October, a new incubation round will launch, bridging grassroots and academic leadership for meaningful learning and growth opportunities

Whether you’re a small business owner just starting out, or a seasoned entrepreneur looking to grow your business, the Napa Chamber of Commerce is here to help you achieve your goals. 

Our value is driven by member engagement and education; economic development; and advocacy. 

Members of the Napa Chamber of Commerce gain access to a range of benefits that help business thrive and are connected to an influential network of like-minded business leaders, employers and employees who are committed to supporting each other’s success. 

When you join the Napa Chamber of Commerce, you become part of a longstanding pillar of the community. Name: Meadville Community Revitalization Corporation
Description:
We work collaboratively with residents, partners, and stakeholders MCRC will implement actionable development strategies for the near and long-term benefit of the entire community. This includes:

1. Implement Strategic Projects: The MCRC will collaborate with community groups to support renovation of existing structures and development of new residential and commercial spaces that contribute to our downtown.

2. Improve Public Spaces:  The MCRC will collaborate with community groups to facilitate place-based enhancements including lighting, sidewalks, greenspace, and public art that supports creative placemaking and placekeeping while resulting in a more attractive and accessible downtown.

3. Support Businesses: The MCRC will support development of a creative, vibrant downtown economy through business retention, resources, and recruitment.

4. Lead Branding and Marketing: The MCRC will promote Meadville as a destination for residents, visitors, and businesses guided by a marketing plan that highlights the unique assets and creates a cohesive identity for the area.

5. Engage the Community: The MCRC will foster connectivity across existing efforts to ensure that community voices are heard and supported through authentic partnerships.

Name: PIEGM - Guinée
Description:
Le Projet d'interconnexion électrique Guinée-Mali (PIEGM) est une initiative qui s'inscrit dans la lignée du Programme d'échange d'énergie électrique ouest-africain (WAPP). Il vise à construire une ligne de transmission électrique sur 714 km entre la Guinée et le Mali. 

Ce projet ambitieux a pour objectif de combler le déficit énergétique en connectant plus de 18 000 foyers, assurant ainsi un approvisionnement électrique fiable et abordable. Avec un financement de plus de 284,7 millions d’euros, l’État guinéen et ses partenaires, notamment l’Union Européenne (UE), la Banque africaine de développement (BAD), la Banque européenne d’investissement (BEI), la Banque mondiale (BM) et la Banque d’investissement et de développement de la CEDEAO (BIDC), affichent une détermination sans faille à développer le secteur énergétique en Guinée. 

Cette initiative permettra d’améliorer la qualité de vie des populations et favoriser une intégration sous-régionale fructueuse.

Name: Industrie und Handelskammer zu Berlin - IHK Berlin
Description:
Die IHK Berlin ist die Organisation der Berliner Unternehmen. Zugegeben – der Zusammenschluss der Unternehmen in der IHK ist keine freiwillige Angelegenheit, sondern gesetzlich vorgeschrieben. Dem Gesetzgeber lag daran, eine Institution der Wirtschaft zu schaffen, die einerseits den Bedürfnissen der Wirtschaft Rechnung trägt, andererseits aber unabhängig vom Einfluss oder speziellen Interessen einzelner Wirtschaftszweige, Branchen oder Unternehmen ist. Eine IHK muss demnach das Interesse aller zu ihr gehörenden Unternehmen im Blick behalten und vor diesem Hintergrund die Wirtschaft ihrer Region insgesamt fördern. Dort wo wirtschaftliche Tätigkeit im Interesse der Allgemeinheit geregelt werden muss, kann die IHK als eigene Einrichtung der Wirtschaft diese Aufgaben übernehmen und so den staatlichen Einfluss auf Unternehmen möglichst gering halten.
Was heißt das für die IHK Berlin konkret?

    Wir fördern den Wirtschaftsstandort Berlin und setzen uns für gute Bedingungen für die Berliner Unternehmen ein. Die Interessen der Berliner Wirtschaft vertreten wir mit breitgefächertem Sachverstand und sind in allen Wirtschaftsfragen kompetenter Gesprächspartner für Senat und Behörden sowie anderer Interessengruppen. Wir äußern uns zu Gesetzes- oder Verwaltungsvorhaben, die die Wirtschaft berühren, wir sind Gutachter oder Berater in Fachausschüssen oder Partner bei vielen unterschiedlichen Initiativen in Berlin.
    Dort wo staatliche Regelung unvermeidbar ist, übernehmen wir hoheitliche Aufgaben, gestalten diese unbürokratisch und erfüllen sie effizient.
    Wir unterstützen die Berliner Unternehmen mit einer Fülle an Dienstleistungen, ohne dabei jedoch zum Konkurrenten für unsere Mitglieder zu werden.

Für die Umsetzung dieser Aufgaben in die praktische tägliche Arbeit sind die Unternehmen Berlins und die Mitarbeiter der IHK gemeinsam verantwortlich. Für alle wirtschaftsrelevanten Fragen kombinieren wir den unternehmerischen Esprit unseres Ehrenamtes und fachspezifischen Sa

Description:
Montana Business Assistance Connection (MBAC) drives economic growth and livability in Lewis and Clark, Broadwater, and Meagher counties. We help businesses start, grow, relocate, or transition through personalized guidance, strategic partnerships, and access to a variety of loan funds.

From mentoring with partners like SBDC and SCORE to connecting businesses with capital they may not otherwise access, we work to reduce risk and unlock opportunity. We believe that change can be created by bringing together individuals from all walks of life who are driven and committed to making a difference in the world. We do this by teaming with leaders in industry, government, and civil society to develop regions. national, and international agendas.

Name: Gundjeihmi Aboriginal Corporation Jabiru Town
Description:
Gundjeihmi Aboriginal Corporation Jabiru Town (GACJT) holds the new township lease over Jabiru and administers sub-leases and licences for the many businesses and government services located in Jabiru. GACJT is an Indigenous corporation whose members are the Mirarr People, the Traditional Owners and Native Title Holders for Jabiru.

The existence of GACJT ensures that income from the township lease benefits the Mirrar’s economic and community development. It is incorporated under the Corporations (Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander) Act 2006 (Cth).

It is essential to GACJT that its strategic direction aligns with and allows for the achievement of the aspirations of the traditional owners to protect culture and to ensure that Bininj are in control of their country and the future of Jabiru.

GACJT’s Board consists of four senior Mirarr directors as well as two independent directors. Its membership is comprised of Mirarr traditional Aboriginal owners.

Name: Indiana First Region
Description: The Indiana First Region identifies itself as the southern gateway and crossroads to the State of Indiana, referred to affectionately by its residents  as the “Rural Piece of Paradise” between the metros of Bloomington, Evansville, and Louisville. The region is rural, connected to one another by interstates and state roads. Indiana First is a region of tremendous assets. Recreation abounds from our rolling hills, meandering streams, navigable rivers, and forested lands, to our theme parks and affordable and active communities that provide high quality of life and diverse housing for current and future residents. Our well-connected infrastructure and robust private investment supports diverse industries and innovative institutions that ensures sustainable living, next generation careers, and an entrepreneurial spirit that raises awareness of the State and attracts multigenerational population to live, work, and play in our region (Regional Economic and Acceleration and Development Strategy).

Our society organizes a diverse range of academic and extracurricular activities, including seminars and guest lectures featuring distinguished economists and industry leaders, skill development workshops on quantitative analysis and research methodologies, and interactive competitions such as quizzes, policy debates, and paper presentations. 

At Artha Kutumb, we actively promote research initiatives that encourage students to explore contemporary economic issues and contribute original insights to academic discourse. Our mission is to inspire analytical thinking, collaborative learning, and innovative problem-solving among future economists and policymakers. By building a vibrant network of knowledge-sharing and professional mentorship, we aim to empower students to navigate the complexities of global economic challenges effectively. Unser Ziel ist es, junge Gründer und Unternehmer zu unterstützen und ihnen eine Plattform zu bieten, um sich zu vernetzen, ihre Ideen und Projekte vorzustellen und von erfahrenen Mentoren und Branchenführern zu lernen.

Name: Bayou Microfund
Description:
Our mission is to incubate micro-entrepreneurs in the Greater Houston area by providing interest-free loans and mentoring.

We have been a non-profit 501(c)(3) corporation and a Kiva Trustee since 2014. In 2021, with support from Kiva and the Michael and Susan Dell Family Foundation, we became the caretaker of Kiva's Houston Hub. From application, to fundraising, to repayment, to technical assistance, we are here to support local entrepreneurs through their Kiva Journey. 

We provide interest-free loans and support by mentoring in different areas to our entrepreneurs to help them achieve their goals. Our loans are funded in two different ways: through direct loans with our Partner Banks and through our Kiva Houston Hub.

We love to hear from others who are passionate about microlending and who wish to make a difference by giving back. Join us as we seek to create a virtuous circle of giving that enlivens a vibrant micro-lending community in Texas.

Name: Vermillion County Economic Development Coalition
Description:
Vermillion County, Indiana is a place where the living is easy, thanks to delightfully affordable housing and remarkably low taxes. More importantly, though, it’s a place where traditional Midwestern values are woven through daily life. It’s the kind of community where kids congregate in 4-H clubs, the FFA chapter, and the basketball court.

Local residents take full advantage of the fresh air and other natural resources, whether in their own backyards or some of Indiana’s well-loved state parks. We’re minutes from Turkey Run, Shades, and Shakamak State Parks. The Wabash River forms our county’s eastern border, and popular recreational waters like Sugar Creek and Raccoon Lake are our neighbors. Whether you spend your spare time fishing, hunting, canoeing, camping, biking, or just enjoying nature’s bounty, you’ll be at home here.

Our communities enjoy big gatherings, and our annual events draw visitors from throughout the Midwest. Clinton’s Little Italy Festival celebrates the food and culture of the ancestral home of many of our residents, and vintage auto enthusiasts put their lovingly restored vehicles to the test at the Newport Antique Auto Hill Climb. Parke County’s storied Covered Bridge Festival is just minutes away.

Are you in search of even more excitement? We’re within driving distance of all the dining, shopping, professional sports, and other entertainment you’ll find in Indianapolis, Chicago, St. Louis, Cincinnati, and Louisville, so you can indulge yourself and come back home to relax. Nearby Lafayette and Terre Haute also have plenty to offer.

You’ll have peace of mind here, too. Crime rates are very low, and our healthcare facilities are first-rate, with access to top specialists close to home.

Name: Africa Centre for Just Transition (ACJT)
Description:
About Us
The Africa Centre for Just Transition (ACJT) is dedicated to ensuring that Africa’s shift to a sustainable, low-carbon economy is fair, inclusive, and people-centered. We work at the intersection of climate action, energy transition, social justice, and economic empowerment, advocating for policies and solutions that uplift communities while driving sustainable development.

Our Mission
We champion equity in the energy transition, ensuring that workers, vulnerable communities, and marginalized groups are not left behind as Africa moves toward a greener future. Through research, advocacy, capacity building, and multi-stakeholder engagement, we create pathways for a just and sustainable transition.

Our Focus Areas
 Policy & Advocacy – Shaping just transition policies that promote economic and social fairness.
 Research & Innovation – Generating data-driven insights to support inclusive climate and energy solutions.
 Community Empowerment – Equipping local communities with the skills and resources to thrive in the green economy.
 Youth & Workforce Development – Preparing Africa’s workforce for the future of sustainable industries.

Join Us
We collaborate with governments, businesses, civil society, and international partners to drive impactful change. Whether you’re a policymaker, researcher, activist, or organization, we invite you to work with us in shaping a just and resilient future for Africa.

 Together, we can transition sustainably—without leaving anyone behind.

Name: Journal of Development Perspectives
Description:
Challenges of economic and social development are, by their nature, diverse. They cover the coordination of action through social and political institutions, as well as actions of individuals and of organizations. Its welfare dimensions are also broad, ranging from narrowly defined economic interests to broader conceptions of human progress. Constraints and enabling mechanisms arise from both human agency, and the environment within which humans exercise their agency. Inevitably, this means that a range of disciplines are relevant to scholars interested in development, from the social sciences such as economics, political science, and sociology, through management disciplines, and an ever-increasing set of the natural sciences, from genetics to environmental sciences and beyond. Following relevant progress across such a wide array of disciplines, each with their specific sets of methodologies and styles of presenting evidence and assessing its robustness, is non-trivial in and of itself, and made more challenging by ever-increasing specialization in each area of enquiry.

The Journal of Development Perspectives intends to provide an opportunity for scholars interested in development to contribute to improved communication across disciplines. It does so by providing a platform both for the publication of new research findings presented in a manner accessible to a generalist audience, and by actively encouraging synoptic assessments of the state of knowledge in areas of interest to scholars of development. In doing so, it invites contributions from all disciplines that are relevant to development questions.

Name: Foro de Debate Económico Germán Bernácer
Description:
El Foro de Debate Económico Germán Bernácer, tiene carácter privado e independiente. Nace por la iniciativa de un grupo de despachos profesionales de la provincia de Alicante, para generar un espacio participativo y abierto que aporte conocimiento y opinión, y que contribuya al progreso económico y social de la provincia de Alicante.

Toma su nombre del ilustre economista y físico alicantino, Germán Bernácer, que fue Catedrático de la Escuela de Comercio de Alicante, Catedrático de Física y Química en la Escuela de Altos Estudios Mercantiles de Madrid y fundador y director del Servicio de Estudios del Banco de España.

Germán Bernácer está considerado uno de los padres de la Macroeconomía, que en el siglo XX desarrolló y promulgó importantes teorías de gran influencia en el desarrollo de la ciencia Económica, y que con sus aportaciones edificó las bases de la macroeconomía moderna.  Fue un investigador  adelantado a su época que a lo largo de su dilatada carrera abordó con acierto numerosas áreas del conocimiento. En su extenso destacan un buen número de libros, artículos y ensayos.

Name: ZeroWasteLIFE
Description:
The core of the problem to be addressed: processing wastes.
The manufacture of composite material parts is deployed through several steps. For the high quality, space or automotive-transportation parts to be realized, the process involves the cutting of a pattern out of pre-pregs or
tows of virgin fabric materials. The cutting is typically done in automated tables and operators are responsible for collecting the cuts and disposing on top of moulds, to prepare the bags and launch the curing process (to be performed in autoclave or under press).
Still, the process for the manufacture of composite parts is heavily relying on trimmed and precision cut parts: in spite of heavy optimization, still an amount estimated between 30% and 50% is wasted along the process (the value of wasted material is estimated in the median 40% within this project).

Name: The Economics Review at ASU
Description:
Who Are We: Founded in 2022, The Economics Review at ASU is Arizona State University's premier peer-reviewed undergraduate economics publication. 

Our organization is fully student-run, with all published content researched, written, and edited by students of all academic backgrounds with a passion for economics. Each piece features in-depth analysis on a pressing economic issue of our time, and showcases students applying both theoretical and empirical strategies to communicate their position.

Our Mission: The ASU Economic Review provides a platform for undergraduate students to communicate their views on economic topics of interest. We help students apply knowledge acquired in the classroom and foster a community of students passionate about economics.

Disclaimer: All views in this publication reflect those of the individual authors, and do not reflect the opinions or policies of The Economics Review at ASU staff, the ASU Department of Economics, or Arizona State University in general.

Name: Wichita Tribe Industrial Development Commission
Description:
The WTIDC serves the 3,850 citizens of the Wichita and Affiliated Tribes. Our profits are used to support our citizens and the local community of our Tribal Jurisdiction.

Historically, the Wichita, Waco, Keechi, and Tawakoni People flourished on the Great Plains. Masters of agrarian systems and skilled in commercial trade, our ancestors lived throughout present day Kansas, Oklahoma, and Texas. Today, our Tribal Jurisdiction is located in the northern half of Caddo County, Oklahoma.

The Wichita Tribe Industrial Development Commission (WTIDC) is the economic development entity of the Wichita and Affiliated Tribes. WTIDC was established under the Wichita Tribe Industrial Development Act of 1992 (Wichita Public Law No. 92-100-001) pursuant to the authority vested in the Wichita and Affiliated Tribes of Oklahoma by Article Five of the Wichita Governing Resolution. WTIDC’s responsibility is to manage and grow the tribe’s commercial, tribal, and federal portfolio and provide viable economic sustainability.

Traditionella evenemang som vi anordnar är Rookie-perioden (vilket är för våra nya studenter och sträcker sig över 2 veckor för att lära känna klasskamrater och skolan), höstsittning, SM-ekonomi, pluggstugor samt studenttorg med olika företag och sponsorer (Esekondagen). 

Vår uppgift är att ekonomstudenterna ska få möjlighet att påverka sitt studentliv för att få det bästa möjliga förutsättningar till att få sitt framtida jobb. 

Esekon är ansluten till Sveriges Ekonomföreningars Riksorganisation (S.E.R.O) som är en paraplyorganisation för ekonomföreningar och kårer. S.E.R.O tillhandahåller tre konferenser varje år innehållande utbildning, erfarenhetsutbyte och möjlighet till ett utökat kontaktnät.

Name: South West Health and Life Sciences Cluster Development Initiative
Description:
The South West Health and Life Sciences Cluster Development Initiative has been established by the GW4 Alliance, Health Innovation West of England, Health Innovation South West and South West Life Sciences. 

We are engaging with stakeholders across the South West to co-produce structured proposals that articulate a collective vision for development of a formal South West Health and Life Sciences Cluster, enabling us to foster strategic collaborations, showcase our regional assets, skills and talents, innovate in health and life sciences, attract inward investment, improve health outcomes, and drive economic growth in the region. 

If you are interested in contributing to these discussions, please sign up to our mailing list: https://mailchi.mp/healthinnowest.net/cluster

We are looking to promote and connect all place-based assets and networks within the region to generate opportunities and advocacy for the whole of the South West.  Whilst we are focusing on health and life sciences, we are keen to explore cross-cutting and disruptive technologies, such as AI, Quantum Computing, Digital, Robotics, Engineering Biology and Defence Medicine, that will drive future solutions for health.   

The key stakeholders we are engaging with include: 

•	Universities and research organisations 
•	Industry – from spinouts and SMEs to global companies 
•	Healthcare providers 
•	Voluntary, community and social enterprise sector   
•	Financial and investment 
•	Business support organisations 
•	Property developers and operators 
•	Local government. 

This is a one-year programme to generate proposals and evidence for the formalisation and operationalisation of a South West Health and Life Sciences Cluster in 2026.

Disability Youth Advocacy Network (DYANZ) is a Zimbabwean private voluntary organization youth-driven force for social change. We champion the socio-economic empowerment and inclusion of young people with disabilities and young women in Zimbabwe's development journey.

 •Empowerment Through Enterprise: We equip young people with disabilities and women with the skills and resources needed to thrive through business entrepreneurship and development, achieving self-sustainability.
 •Amplifying Voices, Building Bridges: Through strategic outreach programs, we mobilize young people with disabilities, fostering a culture of inclusion and participation in decision-making processes.
DYANZ is:
 •A catalyst for change: We advocate for policies that remove barriers and ensure 
 equal access to education, employment, and leadership opportunities.
 •A champion for self-sufficiency: We provide skills development and resources to empower young people with disabilities and young women to build successful businesses and livelihoods.
  •A platform for inclusion: We amplify the voices of young people with disabilities and women, ensuring they are heard, valued, and included in shaping a brighter future for all.

Partner with DYANZ to invest in a future where every young person, regardless of ability, has the power to reach their full potential.

Name: Southern Ohio Diversification Initiative (SODI)
Description:
SODI is designated by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) as the local community reuse organization. SODI receives personal property and clean and remediated land from the DOE former Portsmouth Gaseous Diffusion Plant (PORTS) in Pike County to leverage in regional economic diversification efforts. 
PORTS was constructed in the early 1950s to enrich uranium for the nation’s nuclear defense and energy missions, and the site was vital in our country’s efforts to fight and win the Cold War. For over 70 years, the site has been a major employer in the region.
SODI’s reindustrialization of PORTS is focused on developing an “all-of-the-above” energy generation complex co-located with high-volume industrial electricity users and manufacturing. This strategy will accelerate power generation resources for Ohio industries, reduce the burden on the national grid, attract billions of dollars in private capital, and produce thousands of well-paying jobs in the private sector. Currently, four industry partners intend to develop projects in hydrogen, ammonia, and silicon production, advanced nuclear small modular reactors, utility-scale battery storage, and high-capacity data processing.
Co-located industries at the PORTS site that are separate from SODI site reindustrialization activities but vital to reindustrialization include Centrus Energy Corp.’s production of High-Assay, Low-Enriched Uranium (HALEU) for use in advanced nuclear small modular reactors and the Depleted Uranium Hexafluoride (DUF6) Conversion facility.
SODI’s reindustrialization plan is conducted in collaboration with DOE’s Office of Environmental Management Portsmouth/Paducah Project Office (DOEEM PPPO) and Ohio University’s Voinovich School PORTSfuture Program (funded by DOE EM PPPO) and is supported by local and regional economic development organizations, Chambers of Commerce, elected officials, labor groups, community members and a multitude of regional stakeholders.

Name: FoodHealth Innovation
Description:
(DE) FoodHealth ist ein Innovationsförderprogramm, welches von den zwei Kantonen Basel-Stadt und Aargau getragen wird und fördert spezifisch Innovationen im Bereich Agri- und Foodtech. Im Fokus stehen High-Tech-Innovationen entlang der gesamten Wertschöpfungskette. Von nachhaltigen Agrartechnologien bis zu gesundheitsfördernden Ernährungslösungen.
Die Nordwestschweiz bietet als führende Region im Bereich Life Sciences ideale Rahmenbedingungen, um transformative und nachhaltige Innovationen zu fördern. Naturwissenschaftliche Erkenntnisse können die Entwicklung marktfähiger Anwendungen begünstigen und Innovationen für einen positiven Impact auf Umwelt und Gesundheit vorantreiben. Bis 2028 fokussiert sich FoodHealth deshalb auf folgende
drei Felder:
-	Das Innovationsökosystem stärken
-	Innovationen in Unternehmen fördern
-	Startups gezielt unterstützen

(EN) FoodHealth is an innovation promotion program supported by the two cantons of Basel-Stadt and Aargau that specifically promotes innovation in the field of agri- and food tech. Our focus is on high-tech innovations along the entire value chain. From sustainable agricultural technologies to health-promoting nutritional solutions.
As a leading region in the field of life sciences, northwestern Switzerland offers ideal conditions for promoting transformative and sustainable innovations. Scientific insights can drive the development of market-ready applications, creating a positive impact on both the environment and human health. Until 2028, FoodHealth will therefore focus on the following three areas:
-	Strengthening the innovation ecosystem
-	Fostering innovation in established companies
-	Offering targeted support for startups

Description:

As part of the nationally recognized Main Street America program, we focus on supporting small businesses, fostering economic development, enhancing neighborhood aesthetics, and creating meaningful connections between residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors.

We believe in the power of community collaboration, local pride, and shared legacy. Our tagline—“A Neighborhood Built by Neighbors. Family Unity Legacy.”—drives everything we do. From district-wide beautification projects and family-friendly events to small business support and strategic advocacy, our goal is to make Curry Ford West a place where everyone feels at home and invested in its future.

What We Do:

Promote and support local small businesses

Organize community events and cultural celebrations

Facilitate economic vitality and business investment

Implement district beautification and public art projects

Strengthen partnerships between residents, businesses, and local government


Join us as we work to create a connected, creative, and thriving Curry Ford West—right in the heart of Orlando.

Learn more at www.curryfordwest.com

Name: Ecostanza
Description:
Ecostanza is a space where economics becomes conversation — one-minute reads, short essays, and research-driven reflections on how economies move, adapt, and impact everyday life.

From inflation trends to fiscal choices, from global trade to behavioural biases, Ecostanza explores the full rhythm of economic activity — combining data, policy, and perspective.

Each piece draws from ongoing research, academic work, and real-world shifts across countries, turning technical ideas into clear, human stories.
Because economics isn’t just theory or numbers — it’s the language of our daily decisions, growth, and resilience.

 Ecostanza | Exploring economies, behaviours, and the world in motion.

Name: Jacksboro EDC
Description:
Jacksboro EDC implements their vision, with an emphasis on land use, transportation, entrepreneurship, tourism and the environment, for the purpose of job creation and increasing the wealth of the people of Jacksboro.

The JEDC Board of Directors, along with Executive Director, Brenda Tarpley, are committed to an economic development strategy that retains the small town values that distinguish Jacksboro life, encourages cooperation between the city and county governments and strives for diversification of the economic base. They continue to set goals and priorities each year, following a strategic plan, that serves as a road map to our successful growth and community improvement.

Name: NMRL Ventures
Description: We invest in ventures using technology to solve traditional problems, rather than simply enhance and support traditional methods.

We facilitate knowledge sharing on a wide range of topics related to economics, including macro and microeconomic theory, public policy, and global economic trends.
Join us in building UAE's most active university-level economics club!

Name: MICROFUTURE ROMANIA
Description:
Proiectul MICROFUTURE - Future proof microfinance for social inclusion

Obiectiv principal - Învățare interregională pentru a îmbunătăți politicile publice care sprijină microfinanțarea ca instrument strategic pentru incluziune socială.

Obiectiv secundar - Sprijinirea îmbunătățirii unui cadru de politici publice care încurajează redresarea socio-economică, inclusiv economia socială, luând în calcul impactul pozitiv și efectul multiplicator al microfinanțării în acest domeniu.

Obiectiv secundar - Creșterea capacității autorităților publice și a furnizorilor de microfinanțare orientați spre componenta socială de a optimiza instrumentele de microfinanțare și alte scheme de sprijin, în conformitate cu noile nevoi ale pieței

Name: Economics Club | Seton Hall
Description: The Economics Club is the social, cultural and academic hub for all Seton Hall University students who are interested in the field of Economics. Students from any background or academic discipline are invited to come and learn from thought leaders in Economics who will foster discussion about a wide range of topics that are relevant to the most pressing issues we face as a society. Our promise to our members is to adhere to the following principles: inclusion, respectful debate, and academic excellence.

Name: Princeton University Economic Development Organization
Description: We are a group of students who constantly question processes we see in the world, looking specifically at the Global South. We have a vested interest in economic development, and we seek to understand how policy impacts people. After posing a specific question, we diligently research to learn about the issue and comprehend the problem in its entirety. Our findings are then compiled into different media, such as a paper, presentation, video, etc., with the hope of publication.

Name: IPADESS
EESCOOP
Description:
La Escuela de Especialización Profesional en Economía Social, Cooperativas y Otras Organizaciones de Participación (EESCOOP), integrada en la Universidad Complutense de Madrid, es un referente en el ámbito de la economía social en España. Su misión es formar, investigar y asesorar en todo lo relacionado con cooperativas, sociedades laborales, mutualidades, fundaciones y otras entidades participativas.

Desde su origen en 1971 como Escuela de Estudios Cooperativos, EESCOOP ha evolucionado hacia un enfoque integral que abarca no solo cooperativas, sino también todas aquellas formas empresariales donde priman la participación, la solidaridad y la sostenibilidad económica y social.

Entre sus funciones destacan:

La formación especializada a través de títulos propios y cursos que capacitan a profesionales, estudiantes y miembros de entidades en dirección y gestión de organizaciones de economía social.

La investigación teórica y aplicada, generando conocimiento y promoviendo proyectos que fortalecen el sector.

El asesoramiento técnico y jurídico a entidades del ámbito participativo, tanto en su constitución como en su desarrollo estratégico.

La colaboración con instituciones públicas y privadas, facilitando la transferencia de conocimiento y fomentando políticas públicas orientadas a la economía social.

EESCOOP apuesta por un modelo económico más justo, inclusivo y democrático, y trabaja activamente para formar líderes capaces de transformar el tejido empresarial desde la cooperación y el compromiso social.

Name: Impact Token Africa
Description:
Impact Token Africa is a platform-in-development that seeks to rewire the economy from below — by recognising, validating, and rewarding the public-good work that young people are already doing across South Africa.
We are not a job creation programme.
We are building a new infrastructure layer that enables systemic investment into the social economy — through verified contributions, dignity-based value flows, and capital orchestration rooted in justice.

Our approach links:

Youth-led public good work (caregiving, learning, food, climate, mental health)

Civil society and community programmes as validators and enablers

Supply-side institutions as partners that plug into live value streams

CSI, private sector, and philanthropic actors as systemic co-investors

Impact Token does not ask for new money.
It asks: Can we circulate existing capital more justly, transparently, and equitably ? 

We are building in public — deliberately and openly — to shape this platform in dialogue with funders, youth, communities, and policymakers alike.

 Based in South Africa
 Contact: rudyo@impacttoken.africa

Name: Lumion
Description:
Lumion är ett enkelt bokföringsprogram för småföretag i Sverige som vill sköta sin bokföring själva utan att behöva förlita sig på dyra konsulter.

Små aktiebolag i Sverige brottas dagligen med bokföring och administration som upplevs som krånglig, tidskrävande och riskfylld. Existerande system är kraftfulla men i första hand byggda för redovisningskonsulter, inte för företagare själva. Resultatet blir att entreprenörer fastnar i komplexa gränssnitt, gör fel eller tvingas betala 20 000 - 50 000 kr per år till byråer för relativt enkla uppgifter. Detta skapar onödig stress, kostnader och en känsla av att inte ha kontroll över bolagets ekonomi.

Vi gör bokföring begriplig och trygg genom guidade flöden och klarspråk, så att företagare slipper betala dyra byråkostnader men ändå kan känna full kontroll.

Description: The San Joaquin Partnership’s focus is business attraction, retention and expansion into and throughout San Joaquin County and the seven incorporated cities of Stockton, Lodi, Manteca, Lathrop, Tracy, Ripon and Escalon. Name: Foggy Bottom West End Main Street
Description:
Businesses in Foggy Bottom and the West End face unique challenges. From rising operational costs to increased competition, local businesses need support to thrive, Many local businesses in Foggy Bottom and the West End are fighting against market shifts, high rental rates, and lack of exposure in a rapidly growing city. Small businesses and local entrepreneurs may not have access to the resources they need to succeed or grow, making it difficult to compete against larger chains.

The Foggy Bottom West End Main Streets (FBWEMS) is here to help businesses in our neighborhood thrive. Through our targeted revitalization efforts, we provide the necessary support to help businesses grow, enhance their visibility, and reach new customers. We also create an inviting environment for residents and visitors alike to enjoy. We help businesses grow and communities thrive by providing resources, advocacy, and opportunities for revitalization, ensuring that local businesses not only survive but flourish within an engaged and vibrant neighborhood.

Name: New Madrid County Economic Development Association
Description: The mission of the New Madrid County Economic Development Association is to foster economic growth and prosperity within the county by attracting new businesses, supporting existing industries, and creating job opportunities for residents. With its strategic location, abundant natural resources, and supportive business environment, New Madrid County offers businesses a competitive edge for success. Whether it's access to transportation networks, a skilled workforce, or a business-friendly climate, the county provides the essential ingredients for businesses to thrive and prosper.

Name: NETWORKS Sullivan Partnership
Description:
NETWORKS Sullivan Partnership is the Economic Development Organization for Sullivan and Hawkins counties, including Bluff City, Bristol, and Kingsport. NETWORKS is governed by a Board of City and County mayors and their appointments. 
Our mission is to ensure that the Tri-Cities and the surrounding counties and cities are the preferred choice for business investment resulting in quality jobs for our citizens and tax base growth for our communities.
Programs
•	Serve as the primary recruitment agent for developing sustainable quality jobs and business investment.
•	Assist in the retention of existing jobs and businesses. 
•	Provide a variety of sites to accommodate new and expanding businesses.
•	Improve collaboration and build local, regional, state, national, and global relationships to enhance economic development.
•	Support role in continuously improving the Tri-Cities and surrounding counties as a well-rounded, environmentally friendly community with quality educational, health care, recreational, and cultural opportunities.

Name: Latino Professionals Connect
Description:
Latino Professionals Connect is dedicated to fostering a supportive network for Latino/a/x professionals and business owners in Eugene, Springfield, and surrounding areas. Through bi-monthly "Night at the Museum" networking events, we create opportunities for individuals to connect, engage, and expand their professional networks. 

Our events provide a welcoming space for collaboration and growth, connecting professionals from diverse backgrounds to drive success and promote community engagement. Join us to build relationships, share insights, and strengthen our vibrant professional community.

Name: Global Adelaide
Description:
Global Adelaide advocates for immigration strategies that drive equitable economic growth across our region. We inform policy makers to shape immigration policy and practice that empowers government, business, industry and the community to create lasting regional change.

As a nonpartisan, not for profit organisation, we are focused on the development, revitalisation and prosperity for all South Australians. We produce nationally recognised immigration research to shape immigration policy and practice through:
1. Generating new ideas
2. Fostering public discussion
3. Informing policy makers.

Our goals are to:
1. Understand the economic value migrants bring to our state
2. Reform immigration programs to:
  * Facilitate population growth
  * Maximise economic value to South Australia
  * Power business and industry with a suitably skilled and experienced workforce
  * Revitalise and support regional communities
3. Attract skilled migrants to South Australia.

Name: BRAVE - Building Blueprints for Regional Clean Energy Scale-up
Description:
13 organisations across Northern Europe have formed the BRAVE partnership to develop seven regional public-private investment ecosystems worth €150m, designing business models and investment blueprints to break out of the "pilot trap" in cleantech. 

Over four years BRAVE's partners, including leaders in local government, academia and business, will trial new ways to scale cleantech solutions - with a focus on increasing ‘business readiness’ in the Netherlands, Belgium, Denmark, France, Sweden, and Germany. 

BRAVE is supported by the European Union's Interreg North Sea funding programme.

Name: ConvergeHER

Be a leader. Be an innovator.

We provide a global leadership community and platform with a reach of over 50,000 to people alike to forge the meaningful connections that define authentic leadership. We move beyond siloed discussions to explore how interconnected systems impact women's lives and opportunities, from economic development and technology to healthcare and social structures.

Our vibrant community is both a think tank and a support network for girls and women building the future at the intersection of economics, STEM, and social innovation.

We are more than a community; we are a catalyst. By converging diverse perspectives, we unlock innovative solutions and create a more equitable and prosperous future for all.

quantum foam studios is a Dayton-based creative operations and strategy firm working alongside founders, small business owners, nonprofits, and community partners to build sustainable, scalable growth.

Collaboration is central to how we work. We build shared systems that connect people, ideas, and capital, strengthening local ecosystems and keeping value circulating within communities. Through partner work, ventures, and events, we bring founders, investors, institutions, and creatives together around long-term resilience.

We’re actively building, partnering, and scaling, working alongside the people shaping what comes next. If you’re interested in creative operatio
