Name: Rock County Jumpstart
Description:
Rock County Jumpstart is a collaborative initiative designed to improve the likelihood of success of minority business owners. Our team has a specific focus on providing culturally-competent, culturally-relevant, and culturally-sensitive services designed to address the specific and unique needs of Black business owners in Rock County. We provide the training, tools, and resources these entrepreneurs need to start, grow, and scale their business.

We collaborate with local and regional organizations that fully support our mission and vision and seek to establish mutually beneficial partnerships that help us achieve our goals.

We advocate on behalf of Black business owners with local business and political leaders and economic development stakeholders to encourage them to establish economic development initiatives that improve access to the capital and resources that are necessary to work towards economic equity for minority business owners in Rock County.

Name: Zona Franca de Gran Canaria
Description:

Se trata de una entidad de derecho público, integrada por representantes de los ayuntamientos implicados en el proyecto (Las Palmas de Gran Canaria y Agüimes), el Ministerio de Hacienda, Gobierno de Canarias, Cabildo Insular de Gran Canaria, Autoridad Portuaria de Las Palmas, Cámara de Comercio y Confederación Canaria de Empresarios.

Las ventajas de formar parte de la Zona Franca de Gran Canaria pasan por:

Exención de aranceles, IGIC (IVA), otros impuestos indirectos (AIEM) y en su caso Impuestos Especiales durante la permanencia de la mercancía en la zona.

Cobro anticipado de las restituciones a la exportación.

No aplicación de medidas de política comercial como contingentes o restricciones.

Posibilidad de introducir cualquier mercancía con independencia de su naturaleza, origen, procedencia o destino, sin limitación en la duración de su estancia.

Simplificación de trámites aduaneros.

Reducción del coste financiero de aportación de avales, en relación a otras figuras aduaneras.

Name: EconUnited
Description:
Most teenagers do not have the vital knowledge of financial literacy they will need in their everyday life like the difference between debit and credit cards, what a credit score is, etc. Economics helps teens figure out how the global market works and could be a major interest for them in college. Business helps teenagers understand how the companies they purchase from function every day. It teaches teens how to start their own businesses and the many different types of businesses there are!

Financial Literacy is a major component of our everyday lives that most high school students do not learn about until they are adults or finished with college. At EconUnited, members have the ability to learn more about economics, finance &business through our online lessons, slideshows, videos, & workshops. Members can also use their knowledge gained from EconUnited while we compete in online competitions hosted by other organizations & by ourselves!

EconUnited welcomes high school students worldwide. We are glad to say we have a diverse group of members from different states in the U.S. & countries internationally. At EconUnited, we hope to continue to help more of our peers learn about economics, finance, & business!

~ 450 Members | Members from 40 Different Countries | Members from 29 States

Name: THRYVE
Description:
About THRYVE
THRYVE is a next-generation medical leasing platform designed to unlock the hidden value of under‑utilised medical devices. We enable clinics, hospitals, laboratories, and healthcare providers to generate new revenue while improving access to essential equipment for the businesses that need it most.
Our mission is simple: turn medical device downtime into measurable value. By empowering healthcare organisations to safely and compliantly lease unused capacity, THRYVE helps reduce capital waste, improve operational efficiency, and support better patient outcomes.

Our Vision
A healthcare ecosystem where essential medical technology is always in use, always accessible, and never wasted.

Our Solution
At THRYVE, we combine smart logistics, secure compliance workflows, and a trusted marketplace model to enable:

 Device Owners
• Earn passive income from devices sitting idle or under-utilised
 • Reduce equipment downtime and justify capital investments
 • Access streamlined logistics, insurance, and compliance support

 Device Seekers
• Access high-quality medical devices without large upfront costs
 • Benefit from flexible leasing options – short-term, long-term, or project-based
 • Reduce operational constraints and scale service delivery faster

What Makes THRYVE Different; 

 Safety & Compliance First
GDP-aligned processes, secure documentation, chain-of-custody verification, and approved logistics partners.

 Specialist Logistics Network
We integrate with both specialist medical couriers and national carriers to ensure safe, fully tracked delivery.

 Full Transparency
Real-time tracking, usage reports, device condition logs, and automated invoicing.

 Designed for Healthcare Growth
Our platform is built for clinics and enterprises looking to reduce waste, optimise resources, and scale intelligently.

Name: BRICklyuch.com
Description:
BRICklyuch: Unlocking the BRICS+ Global Agenda

BRICklyuch is the premier platform for professional insights, synthesis, and analysis on the expanded BRICS+ coalition and its impact on the Global South. Our mission is to provide the key (kljuch) understanding needed to navigate the evolving global economic and political architecture.

The BRICS+ alliance, now representing a significant portion of the world's population and economic output (surpassing the G7 in GDP PPP), is not just an intergovernmental forum—it’s a dynamic force committed to systemic global change.

What We Cover:

We break down the consensus and strategic initiatives across BRICS+'s three core pillars:

    Economic & Financial Resilience: Analyzing the rise of the New Development Bank (NDB), advancements in de-dollarization and local currency trade, and the collective strategy to strengthen financial autonomy for emerging markets.

    Global Governance Reform: Tracking the push for a more representative and equitable world order, including necessary reforms of the UN, IMF, and World Bank to reflect the interests of the Global South.

    Sustainable & Digital Future: Synthesizing cooperation on SDGs, clean energy transition, AI governance, and bridging the digital divide across member nations.

If your work intersects with geopolitics, global trade, development finance, or emerging markets, follow BRICklyuch to access expert resumes, in-depth summaries, and strategic intelligence on the forces reshaping our world.

#BRICklyuch #BRICS+ #GlobalSouth #MultipolarWorld #NDB #DeDollarization #GlobalGovernance #EmergingMarkets

Name: HomeGrown Apprenticeships
Description:
Catalyst (KAT-uh-list): A person or thing that prompts change or action.

Are you a business leader in your community? As a leader, you know your community depends upon you for well-paying jobs. At the same time, you anguish over the challenge of meeting market demands, fulfilling orders, staying competitive, and growing while trying to fill the endless list of open positions in your company.

Sound familiar? We get it! We believe we can help.

HomeGrown Apprenticeships - we catalyze and equip business leaders to start and run local, organic apprenticeship programs in their local community. Leaders seeking to [finally] take ownership of the talent shortage in their businesses and community now have a partner to help launch and run a time-tested solution - apprenticeships.

If your business seeks to fill those impossible roles in technology, cybersecurity, project management, digital marketing, AI, websites, analytics, and management—or for companies that rely upon skilled trades—apprenticeships are a perfect fit! Apprenticeships are no longer just for skilled trades.

What are local, organic apprenticeships? Local: paid apprenticeships in businesses in your community. Organic: apprenticeships created by and run by companies in your community. 

What would change if you launched an organic apprenticeship program in your community? What if high school graduates had a line of sight to success for lucrative careers with businesses in their community? What if robust career opportunities that keep graduates near preserved your community's values? What if some significant portion of graduates from the next class from the local high school could hardly wait to work in your community's businesses?

We are looking for Catalysts who say, "Yesterday was the best day to plant a tree; today is the second best. Let's solve this problem!"

Are you a Catalyst? Drop us a note and let’s discuss.

Name: McKinney Rotary Club
Description:
About McKinney Rotary
 
Founded in 1919, the Rotary Club of McKinney has a rich history of personifying the Rotary International motto – “Service Above Self”. This is evidenced by the many programs and organizations we support through community service, promoting youth services and local/international mission projects.
 
Projects that support “Service Above Self”
McKinney Rotary Flag Program: Rotary’s largest fundraiser in support of our community, local and international projects. For a yearly, nominal fee, Rotarian volunteers will come to your home or business and install a flag for major national holidays. The flags will be picked up and stored between holidays. Show your patriotism and sign up today on the website or email below.
 
Rotary Parade of Lights: The annual Rotary Parade of Lights is the McKinney and surrounding communities holiday season opener.  This festive parade, supported by sponsors and volunteers, has over 70 floats, marching bands and ends with the arrival of Santa Claus! Parade date and participant information is available on the website or email below.
 
Visit Rotary and Get Involved: McKinney Rotary Club meets at Rick’s Chophouse in historic downtown McKinney, 107 North Kentucky Street, McKinney Tx. 75069 every Friday at 12:00 pm. For membership or donation information, contact our club on the website or email below.
 
Who Are Rotarians:   
Rotarians are a global network of 1.4 million neighbors, friends, leaders, and problem-solvers who see a world where people unite and take action to create lasting change – across the globe, in our communities, and in ourselves.
 
Solving real problems takes real commitment and vision. For more than 110 years, Rotary's people of action have used their passion, energy, and intelligence to take action on sustainable projects. From literacy and peace to water and health, we are always working to better our world, and we stay committed to the end.

Name: DO-AIR Data Observatory of the Adriatic and Ionian Region
Description:
DO-AIR – Adriatic-Ionian Regional Data Observatory is a strategic initiative launched in December 2024 to provide a comprehensive, data-driven understanding of the Adriatic-Ionian Macroregion. It serves as a permanent platform for monitoring and analyzing key socio-economic and infrastructure trends, with the aim of supporting evidence-based policymaking and enhancing macroregional integration.

DO-AIR focuses on four main areas of research:
- Trade Flows
- Foreign Direct Investments (FDI)
- Business Mapping and Demographics
- Maritime and Air Traffic

The observatory is the result of a joint collaboration between the Forum of the Adriatic and Ionian Chambers of Commerce, the Marche Region, the Chamber of Commerce of the Marche, and the Marche Polytechnic University – through its Interdepartmental Research Centre on Innovation and Entrepreneurship (CIRI). This partnership ensures a high level of scientific rigor and policy relevance.

DO-AIR was officially acknowledged in the Ministerial Declaration of Crete (May 7, 2025) as a best practice within the EU Strategy for the Adriatic-Ionian Region (EUSAIR). It is designed to operate as a structured, continuous monitoring system, providing updated insights that can help regional stakeholders make informed decisions and shape effective strategies.

Through its data-driven approach, DO-AIR strengthens the role of the Marche Region in internationalization and territorial cooperation, while offering tools that promote sustainable development, regional competitiveness, and cross-border collaboration.

Follow us to explore our latest reports and discover how DO-AIR contributes to building a more connected, resilient, and evidence-based Adriatic-Ionian region.

Name: TREDC - Tule River Economic Development Corporation
Description:
It is the mission of the Tule River Economic Development Corporation to diversify our revenue base, create jobs and educate our people to revitalize our community and bring economic success to the tribe.

The Tule River Economic Development Corporation is a Section 17 Federal Corporation chartered by the Tule River Tribal Council of the Tule River Band of Yokuts Indians. The original intent was to create an organization tasked with developing jobs as well as creating a diverse revenue base to benefit the people of the Tule River Reservation and the surrounding community.

At a time when many tribes focused on gaming, the Tule River Indian Tribe chose to utilize its gaming resources to increase further economic expansion.

Having started Tule River Economic Development Corporation years prior to the advent of Indian gaming, it is now situated to help the Tribe diversify its revenue potential as well as employment opportunities for members of the Tribe. Tule River Economic Development Corporation is setting the standard for economic development among Indian tribes across the nation.

To meet the demand of its mission, The Tule River Economic Development Corporation is currently involved in several ventures. While each project is diverse in nature, each takes advantage of relative advantages and resources unique to the Tule River Indian Reservation and the surrounding area.

The Pietermaritzburg & Midlands Chamber of Business (PMCB) is excited to launch the PMCB Global Export Accelerator Programme (GEAP) supported by the Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH
 
Objective:
The GEAP has an overall development objective, that of supporting export-led economic activity, to unlock entrepreneurial opportunities for emerging Black businesses and create export-orientated employment. 

The programme aims to stimulate job creation and income generation for 40 SMEs by providing them with a suit of export orientated development support, ranging from training, mentoring, product development and international market research, to international market access and business linkages.
 
How it will work:
The process and criteria involved in selecting the SMEs is key to the programme’s success. Export development is not for start-ups and those selected must be trading in the domestic market. 

How to apply: 
Complete our application form on the PMCB Website no later than the 31st of March 2022.

Applications are open to successful black-owned businesses that have been in operation for more then a year.

Name: CORECON-PB
Description:
Compete ao Conselho Regional de Economia, conforme determina a Lei Federal N.º 1.411/51, regulamentada pelo Decreto N.º 31.794/52, com nova redação dada pela Lei N.º 6.021/74, Lei N.º 6.537/78, e Resoluções do Conselho Federal de Economia, registrar, disciplinar e exercer a fiscalização da profissão do Economista.

A missão básica da fiscalização é a verificação da regularidade formal do registro, pelo efetivo exercicio da profissão, em defesa da sociedade, tanto das pessoas físicas no exercício de cargo ou emprego, ou como autônomo, como das pessoas jurídicas na prestação de serviços a terceiros.

Constatada a ausência de registro, o Departamento de Fiscalização instaura o Processo Administrativo de Fiscalização, efetuando contato amigável com a parte interessada por meio de ofício, no qual lhe apresenta toda a fundamentação legal e os esclarecimentos acerca da obrigatoriedade de registro.

Persistindo a irregularidade, na forma das instruções regulamentares em vigor, a parte é notificada e, posteriormente, é lavrado o Auto de Infração pertinente por descumprimento dos artigos 14 e 18 da Lei 1411/51.

A ausência de adoção das medidas corretivas pela parte interessada ensejará a aplicação de penalidade pecuniária pelo CORECON, com fundamento no art. 19 da mesma Lei.

Name: Black Hawk Economic Development, Inc.
Description:
Black Hawk Economic Development, Inc. (BHED) is a non-profit economic development organization that provides economic development assistance for business start-up, expansion, and/or retention   projects. The organization was founded in 1978 in response to the challenging regional economic climate of the late 1970’s and 80’s. It has since evloved into a multi-faceted economic development organization steeped in tradition and poised to meet the challenges of the 21st century economy.

Black Hawk Economic Development, Inc. offers two loan programs that provide financial assistance to small businesses in the State of Iowa.

The SBA 504 Loan Program is an economic development program designed to facilitate capital investment and job creation and/or retention. Eligible projects include acquisition of fixed assets, building improvements, and equipment with a minimum useful life of 10 years. Long-term (up to 20 years), favorable fixed interest rate financing is available for up to 90% of the cost of a qualifying project.

The two Revolving Loan Fund (RLF) Programs that provide financing at favorable interest rates to facilitate small business startups, expansion and/or retention projects. RLF financing is typically subordinate to bank financing and makes up the "gap" or short-fall in the required equity portion of a project.  Eligible uses of funds include fixed assets acquisition and working capital.

Our team consists of:

- Educational assistants, students from renowned economic faculties, offering guidance to participants.

Each participant has the opportunity to attend ILE SES for free or receive a partial discount by winning the grant competition.

We operate through a collaborative hub-and-spoke model, using innovative public and private partnerships to support small businesses, investors, and community leaders to generate long-term, sustainable impact.

Our work integrates ancient wisdom with modern strategies to address the complex needs of underserved populations. From blockchain-backed investment platforms to food sovereignty initiatives and digital infrastructure development, we focus on holistic community wellness that bridges faith with practical, measurable solutions. 

Through our Business Development Pipeline and Kingdom Business Academy, we equip entrepreneurs with economic literacy, social impact skills, and access to funding and private equity.

With a leadership team holding over 45 years of federal and community development experience and a portfolio spanning all 50 U.S. states and territories, 

The Kingdom Collective Group is uniquely positioned to scale purpose-aligned initiatives. We serve more than 25 communities and support over 150 businesses through ethical investment, coaching, and governance rooted in transparency, accountability, and faith integration.

By combining land restoration, innovation, biblical frameworks, and economic development, we’re building a movement—not just a model. We invite visionaries, entrepreneurs, and investors to join us in restoring economies, empowering families, and transforming futures—one community at a time.

Om du vill förgrena ditt företagande för en mer hållbar tillväxt är du välkommen att höra av dig till oss. När tiderna känns svåra ska man komma ihåg, stormar gör så att träden söker djupare rötter.

Name: BDCC (Brattleboro Development Credit Corporation)
Description:
Brattleboro Development Credit Corporation (BDCC) is a nationally recognized powerhouse in rural economic development. Our mission is to invest in the drivers of the economy so that people, businesses, and communities of Southern Vermont can thrive. 

We’re here to make a difference. Whether you’re an aspiring entrepreneur, a local business owner, a passionate community member, or a student looking for your career pathway, we believe in the power of people to drive economic success. We offer:

•	Business Support: Offering entrepreneurship support, technical assistance, space to rent, access to capital, succession planning and growth opportunities for businesses of all sizes.

•	Community Development: Collaborating with local partners to improve the quality of life across our region.

•	Workforce Development: Connecting employers with talent and people with opportunities.  BDCC is helping both incumbent workers and our next generation maximize their potential with our rural workforce development connections. 

•	Development Services: From broadband and brownfields, to water lines and factory expansions, BDCC helps critical projects get done.

BDCC has become an essential resource for economic growth in Southeastern Vermont. We combine bold financing, real estate opportunities, and hands-on technical support to create an environment where businesses can thrive.  

We know that vibrant communities are built by those who dream big, work hard, and lift each other up.

Let’s build the future together: Whether you're an entrepreneur, business owner or manager, community leader, or just passionate about Vermont, reach out to us to see how we can work together to create opportunities and make our community even stronger.

BDCC is affiliated with the State of Vermont as the certified Regional Development Corporation (RDC) serving the greater Windham County area, one of 12 certified RDCs throughout Vermont.

Name: Master Expertise Economique du Développement (EED) - IEDES
Description:
Master 2 Etudes du développement – Parcours Expertise Économique du Développement : Politiques publiques et projets (Institut d'études du développement de la sorbonne (IEDES) – Université Paris 1 Panthéon-Sorbonne) 
Sous la direction de Monsieur Mohamed Ali Marouani

Fondé en 1957 et rattaché à Paris 1 Panthéon-Sorbonne depuis 1969, l’IEDES constitue l’un des pôles historiques majeurs de la formation et de la recherche en développement.

Le parcours forme des spécialistes capables d’analyser et d’évaluer les politiques publiques et projets de développement grâce à :
•  une maîtrise des méthodes quantitatives (économétrie, analyse de données, évaluation d’impact, modélisation) ;
•  une expertise en analyse et gestion de projet.

Le diplôme prépare à des carrières au sein des organisations internationales, des institutions publiques, des agences de développement, du secteur privé, ainsi qu’à la recherche appliquée.

Name: Startup Challenge
Description:
Startup Challenge is a new program focused on providing founders and early-stage teams with a dedicated space for experimenting and getting traction. 

The Startup Challenge is a weekend long experiential learning event which will run from Friday night through Sunday night.

The Startup Challenge is actually two competitions in one. We have the Catalyst Cohort and the Traction Cohort, and we have amazing prizes for both!

The Catalyst Cohort is for brand new ideas, and anyone is welcome to participate — even if you don’t have an idea. No startup or special skills required. If you’re wondering if the Catalyst Challenge right for you, it probably is. In the Catalyst Challenge, you’re competing to launch a startup.

The Traction Cohort is for startups that already have some work product. You can have a product or customers, though you certainly don’t have to. But you cannot have raised investment yet. In the Traction Challenge, you’re competing to find traction.

And here’s the kicker. Both challenges are about what you actually get done over the weekend — because talk is cheap, and we don’t care what you accomplished beforehand. So everyone is on an equal playing field.

Name: JobsFlorida Inc.
Description:
JobsFlorida Inc. is a Florida 501(c)(4) social-welfare organization focused on economic development, commercial-corridor revitalization, and high-impact job creation across Miami-Dade and the State of Florida.
We partner with public agencies, local districts, anchor property owners, and employers to improve the built environment, support business activity, and strengthen communities through targeted, mission-driven programs.
Our mandate includes:
• Commercial Corridor Revitalization – façade improvements, streetscape upgrades, retail activation, and district-level improvement programs that increase foot traffic, safety, and economic activity.
• Workforce & Talent Development – employer partnerships, upskilling pipelines, sector-based training programs, and community job creation initiatives.
• Infrastructure & Public Realm Support – water and broadband access, right-of-way improvements, multimodal connectivity, energy reliability, and resilience-focused upgrades.
• Small Business & Retail Support – technical assistance, activation strategy, permitting guidance, and initiatives that help local businesses grow and hire.
• Property & Land-Use Initiatives – site readiness, revitalization planning, public-benefit real estate initiatives, and collaboration with owners on district-wide improvements.
JobsFlorida operates as a community development and public-benefit platform, aligning private-sector capabilities with public-sector goals. We support projects that enhance quality of life, expand economic opportunity, and generate durable employment throughout Florida’s most important commercial corridors — including Downtown Miami and the Flagler District.
Our work is guided by transparency, measurable impact, and partnerships that advance long-term community outcomes.
Contact: 786-494-6766

Name: Integrated Regional Employability & Skills (IRES)
Description:
The Integrated Regional Employability and Skills (IRES) Programme, part of the Edinburgh & South East Scotland City Region Deal, is dedicated to advancing regional labour market policy and practice for inclusive growth. 

With seven themes, the programme has an extensive network of partners including six local authorities, educational institutions, businesses, and non-profit organisations who collaborate to address skill demands and opportunities for regional growth. 

Securing £25 million in Scottish Government funding for eight years, IRES aims to enhance the skills for 14,700 people, support 5,300 people into employment, and support 500 people to advance their careers. Now in its fifth year of delivery, the IRES program is exceeding its targets and objectives across its seven themes:

•	Data Skills Gateway
•	Housing Construction and Infrastructure Skills Gateway 
•	Integrated Employer Engagement 
•	Intensive Family Support Service 
•	Workforce Mobility 
•	Labour Market Analysis and Evaluation 
•	Integrated Knowledge Management Systems

Name: Initiative for Policy Dialogue at Columbia University
Description:
The Initiative for Policy Dialogue works to broaden dialogue and explore trade-offs in development policy by bringing the best ideas in development to policymakers facing globalization’s complex challenges and opportunities. We strive to contribute to a more equitably governed world by democratizing the production and use of knowledge.

Founded in July 2000 by Nobel Laureate Joseph Stiglitz, the Initiative for Policy Dialogue (IPD) stimulates a heterodox policy dialogue on major issues in international development. IPD is a global network of leading economists, political scientists, and premier academic and policy centers in the global South and North. We bring the issues of developing countries to academics and the fruits of academic research to policymakers.

We promote more inclusive and pluralist discourse on the major global debates on economic and political development, in which countries often face a narrow set of policy prescriptions that may not fully suit their domestic preferences and capabilities. IPD helps countries analyze the complex policy trade-offs they face and provides them with access to a broader range of economic solutions that promote democratic, equitable and sustainable development.

Name: L'Economique ESCP
Description:
L'Économique ESCP est l'association d'économie de ESCP Business School. 

Fondée en 2023, il s'agit de la toute première association d'économie a avoir été créée dans l'histoire des écoles de commerce.

L'Économique ESCP a pour but de démocratiser la réflexion et le débat économique au sein de ESCP Business School en s'appuyant sur trois activités principales :

• La première consiste à inviter des économistes et intervenants extérieurs afin d'organiser des conférences et des tables rondes au sein de l'école.
• La deuxième consiste à rédiger des articles d’actualité économique et d’approfondissement visant les promotions de ESCP Business School, les élèves de classe préparatoire ou, plus généralement, toute autre personne s'intéressant aux principaux débats économiques contemporains.
• La troisième consiste à l’organisation de débats afin de favoriser le partage de connaissances et d’opinions sur des sujets économiques donnés.

L'Économique ESCP s'engage tout particulièrement à promouvoir la réflexion, le partage et l'innovation au sein de ESCP Business School.

Das Förderprogramm Basel2037 unterstützt Ihr KMU im Kanton Basel-Stadt dabei, nachhaltiger zu werden und aktiv zur Erreichung der kantonalen Klimaziele beizutragen. Gleichzeitig vermittelt Basel2037 das nötige Praxiswissen und bieten Ihrem KMU gezielte Orientierung zu Nachhaltigkeit, Klimaschutz und Netto-Null.

Der Gewerbeverband Basel-Stadt und der Kanton Basel-Stadt haben den Verein Basel2037 gemeinsam ins Leben gerufen, um KMU gezielt auf ihrem Weg zu Netto-Null zu begleiten und zu unterstützen.

Name: Synergies du Pastel

 Notre mission ? Identifier et développer des coopérations concrètes entre entreprises, artisans, collectivités : mutualisation de services, valorisation des déchets, achats groupés, récupération d’énergie, logistique partagée… pour créer de la valeur locale, réduire les coûts et impulser une dynamique circulaire ambitieuse.

 Qui sommes-nous ? Un collectif actif rassemblant des industriels, PME, collectivités et institutions publiques engagés sur le territoire longeant l’A68 : Tryfil, Decoset, SPLA Portes du Tarn, GRDF, EDF, Reflex Répartition Pharma, Rigal Group, Socotrap, et bien d'autres.

 Ce que nous proposons :

- Un accompagnement structuré et localisé
- Des ateliers de détection de synergies
- L’appui au montage de projets et financements
- Un réseau de partenaires publics-privés impliqués

 Rejoignez une dynamique concrète, collective et tournée vers l’avenir.

#ÉconomieCirculaire #Territoire #EIT #TransitionÉcologique #SynergiesDuPastel

Name: L'Économique ESCP
Description:
L'Économique ESCP est l'association d'économie de ESCP Business School. 

Fondée en 2023, il s'agit de la toute première association d'économie a avoir été créée dans l'histoire des écoles de commerce.

L'Économique ESCP a pour but de démocratiser la réflexion et le débat économique au sein de ESCP Business School en s'appuyant sur trois activités principales :

• La première consiste à inviter des économistes et intervenants extérieurs afin d'organiser des conférences et des tables rondes au sein de l'école.
• La deuxième consiste à rédiger des articles d’actualité économique et d’approfondissement visant les promotions de ESCP Business School, les élèves de classe préparatoire ou, plus généralement, toute autre personne s'intéressant aux principaux débats économiques contemporains.
• La troisième consiste à l’organisation de débats afin de favoriser le partage de connaissances et d’opinions sur des sujets économiques donnés.

L'Économique ESCP s'engage tout particulièrement à promouvoir la réflexion, le partage et l'innovation au sein de ESCP Business School.

Name: CAPDDIL Madagascar
Description:
Le CAPDDIL (Centre d’Appui et de Pilotage du Développement Durable et des Initiatives Locales) est un programme structurant porté par le CEFD Madagascar, dédié à la mise en œuvre concrète du développement durable à l’échelle locale. Implanté au cœur de la commune d’Andoharanofotsy, le CAPDDIL se positionne comme un hub territorial de transition écologique, d’innovation sociale et d’autonomisation économique.

Sa mission est de mobiliser, former, accompagner et relier les acteurs locaux – citoyens, jeunes, associations, agents publics, entrepreneurs, établissements scolaires – autour d’actions pilotes de développement durable. Le centre articule ses interventions autour de plusieurs axes clés :

Éducation citoyenne et environnementale (ODD, éco-responsabilité, mobilisation communautaire),

Formation des agents communaux et des leaders locaux pour une gouvernance durable,

Incubation de projets verts générateurs de revenus (agriculture urbaine, recyclage, énergies renouvelables…),

Renforcement du tissu partenarial local et national pour la pérennisation des actions.


Le CAPDDIL fonctionne comme un accélérateur de solutions locales, avec une approche intégrée mêlant animation territoriale, transfert de compétences, expérimentation et co-construction. Il ambitionne de devenir un modèle réplicable à l’échelle d’autres communes de Madagascar et d’Afrique.

Fruit d’une dynamique partenariale réunissant collectivités locales, associations de terrain, entreprises responsables et institutions académiques (dont l’IEPAG), le projet est conçu sur une durée de trois ans (2024–2027), avec une première phase pilote en cours.

Le CAPDDIL incarne la conviction que le développement durable n’est pas une option, mais une urgence partagée. Il agit pour un futur plus résilient, inclusif et respectueux des ressources locales.

Name: Stanly Small Business Center
Description:
The Stanly Small Business Center is dedicated to supporting the development of new businesses and fostering the growth of existing ones. As a proud member of the NC Community College System Small Business Center Network (SBCN), our services are offered at no cost to the local community and include:

Counseling: Our experienced counselors offer confidential, personalized guidance for prospective and existing small business owners.

Resources and Referrals: Access valuable resources and referrals to subject matter experts for your small business journey.

Training Events: We provide community-based training to equip small business owners with the skills needed for success.

Each semester, the SCC Small Business Center offers seven online, two-hour core classes covering essential topics, from starting a business in North Carolina to managing your bookkeeping. We're excited to now offer the Small Business Startup Certificate to those who complete all seven classes, regardless of whether they are taken in different semesters. This certification is completely free and is designed to provide you with the tools to start, build, and grow your small business!
 
To enroll, simply attend each of the seven core classes, all of which are offered each semester. These classes are listed below, and each class description will note that it satisfies the certification requirement. 

- How to Start a Business
- Financing Your Small Business
- How to Write a Business Plan
- How to Find Your Customers
- Basics of Bookkeeping
- Your Small Business Taxes
- Marketing Your Business

For more information, visit stanly.edu/sbc. If you have any questions, email Cassidy Ball at cball7094@stanly.edu or call 704-991-0355 for more details.

Name: PipeDreamers Foundation
Description:
The PipeDreamers Foundation, founded in 2021 as a 501(c)3 social enterprise under the Stanford Accelerator for Learning, is dedicated to advancing society by focusing on ecosystem development. This includes directing resources into marginalized communities and households impacted by the justice system, with a strategic emphasis on technology, education, and entrepreneurship.

The foundation's core mission is to empower justice-impacted households through various educational and developmental programs. These services are designed to equip individuals with the necessary skills and knowledge to thrive in today's technology-driven world.

A significant aspect of the PipeDreamers Foundation's approach is its pivot into ecosystem development, which involves creating an integrated network of education, technology, and industry partnerships. This holistic strategy aims to foster sustainable growth, innovation, and opportunity within communities.

Key initiatives of the foundation include sustaining technology education programs with partners like Google, Salesforce, Coursera, Stanford, and Cornell; launching accelerator programs in entrepreneurship and innovation in collaboration with Stanford Accelerator for Learning; and enhancing employment opportunities through collaborations with Silicon Valley partners, and employer internships/apprenticeships.

Furthermore, the foundation is actively engaged in ecosystem development for municipal government economic development agencies, facilitating sponsored programs to support community growth and prosperity. Overall, the PipeDreamers Foundation envisions creating a thriving environment where individuals can overcome barriers and achieve their full potential, making positive contributions to their communities and society at large.

Name: NKM Global
Description:
NKM Global envisions a future where South Africa and the entire African continent experience sustained economic growth, leading to prosperity and development for all it's inhabitants by 2050. We aim to have at least 85% of Africa developed, with thriving economies, vibrant communities, and abundant opportunities for growth and advancement. 

Economic Growth Strategy Investment Thesis And Risks 

The high-level investment thesis for NKM Global's vision revolves around the belief that strategic initiatives in property development, logistics, and farming can be leveraged to drive inclusive economic growth, create employment opportunities, and foster sustainable development across Africa. Here's a breakdown of the investment thesis and associated risks:

Investment Thesis:
* 		Property Development:
    * Thesis: Investing in infrastructure development and urban renewal projects can create modern, liveable spaces that attract investment and stimulate economic activity.
    * Risks: Market demand fluctuations, regulatory hurdles, construction delays, and financing risks could impact project viability.
* 		Logistics:
    * Thesis: Investing in transportation and logistics infrastructure can improve connectivity, facilitate trade, and enhance economic efficiency.
    * Risks: Regulatory challenges, geopolitical instability, and technological disruptions may affect the effectiveness and profitability of logistics operations.
* 		Farming:
    * Thesis: Supporting smallholder farmers and promoting sustainable agricultural practices can increase productivity, improve livelihoods, and drive agricultural value addition.
    * Risks: Climate change, market price volatility, access to financing, and regulatory constraints could pose challenges to the viability of farming initiatives.

Navigating these risks while executing the proposed strategies will be critical for NKM Global to achieve its vision of sustainable economic development in South Africa and Africa.

Name: NudgeIran
Description:
Nudge: “Improving Decisions about Health, Wealth, and Happiness” is a book written by University of Chicago economist and Nobel Laureate “Richard H. Thaler” and Harvard Law School Professor “Cass R. Sunstein“, first published in 2008.
The book draws on research in psychology and behavioral economics to defend libertarian paternalism and active engineering of choice architecture. The book also popularized the concept of “nudge theory“.
 A nudge, according to Thaler and Sunstein is any form of choice architecture that alters people’s behavior in a predictable way without restricting options or significantly changing their economic incentives. To count as a mere nudge, the intervention must require minimal intervention and must be cheap.

Name: LAMO - Interreg IPA Adrion
Description:
Programme: Interreg IPA Adrion 2021-2027
Programme priority 2: A greener, low-carbon, and resilient Europe
Lead partner: Municipality of Pella (GR) 

The project unites six municipalities from the Adriatic–Ionian area in a common mission — to promote cycling as a sustainable and active form of transportation.

 The municipalities have joined forces to address a shared question:

How can local authorities actively contribute to the promotion of cycling as a key mode of mobility in the Adriatic–Ionian region?

 Recognizing their essential role in shaping urban spaces, local authorities are committed to improving the infrastructure, accessibility, and visibility of cycling within their cities. Cycling is not only an efficient and affordable means of transport — it also reduces car dependency, eases pressure on urban transport systems, and allows people to explore and connect with their environment in an active, enjoyable way.

 Thanks to the rise of electromobility, cycling is becoming even more accessible, inclusive, and appealing for citizens of all ages and abilities. Beyond its social and health benefits, the shift toward cycling also contributes significantly to lower CO₂ emissions and a cleaner, greener urban environment.

 The project’s overall goal is to support cycling as a sustainable mobility option by establishing a strong transnational network of municipalities and implementing six pilot actions—one in each city—to test and demonstrate innovative cycling solutions.

 The initiative aims to benefit all citizens, including visitors, commuters, and people with disabilities, promoting more equitable access to mobility.

 In line with the EUSAIR Strategy, the project strengthens cooperation and policy innovation across borders, contributing to a more connected, sustainable, and inclusive Adriatic–Ionian region.

Wall, SD is an excellent place to start and grow a business. Our homegrown entrepreneurs have launched businesses like the famous Wall Drug and the Wall Meat Processing Plant. All of Wall’s businesses benefit from the active support of Wall Economic Development and the residents. This support, combined with a low cost of living and low cost of doing business, have made Wall the perfect place to launch or expand a business. Plus, we’re in the geographic center of the U.S., making us the ideal spot to reach customers nationwide.

Living here provides numerous advantages. It’s affordable, our schools are great and we are adjacent to some of the most stunning vistas in the United States. The Badlands National Park is just minutes away, as is fishing, boating, biking, hiking and local golf courses. Young families and retirees alike appreciate our safe and friendly community, beautiful outdoor spaces, and the opportunity to thrive somewhere beautiful. Come join us in Wall, SD - a great place to call home!

Name: Advanced Cap Access
Description:
Advanced Cap Access promotes the economic welfare of low-income communities across the country by providing investment capital to qualified small businesses. 

ACA operates two business financing programs:

(1) The federal New Markets Tax Credit Program which provides tax credits to investors for qualifying investments into Community Development Entities (CDEs).  

(2) The ACA Small Business Micro Loan Fund program which offers loans to qualifying small businesses at competitive interest rates.

Name: Buna Regional Economic Development LLC
Description:
Buna Regional Economic Development LLC (Buna REDI) is a private, self-funded economic development initiative focused on increasing visibility, readiness, and informed engagement for Buna and rural Jasper County, Texas.

Founded by John Hargrove, P.E., with affiliates Will Withers and Tricia Jagneaux, Buna REDI operates where no formal public economic development authority currently exists. Its role is not to govern, negotiate incentives, or commit public resources, but to serve as a connector, signal amplifier, and early-stage filter for opportunities affecting the community.

Buna REDI works to:

Monitor and interpret regional, state, and external economic development activity

Provide realistic, on-the-ground context for rural sites, infrastructure, workforce, and land use

Connect local stakeholders with appropriate regional and state partners

Promote fit-first, values-aligned development that respects rural character and capacity

The organization collaborates informally with chambers of commerce, regional planning bodies, workforce organizations, and county leadership to ensure that rural perspectives are present early in conversations—before decisions are made elsewhere.

Buna REDI is intentionally lean, transparent, and disciplined. It does not claim official designation or exclusive authority. Its value lies in clarity, credibility, and restraint—helping communities avoid misaligned projects while staying visible to the right opportunities.

Focus areas include: rural visibility, infrastructure awareness, workforce alignment, broadband and utilities context, site readiness, and regional coordination.

Buna REDI exists to ensure that rural places are not overlooked—and that when opportunity comes, it fits.

Name: African Institute for Labor Economics and Development
Description:
The African Institute for Labor Economics & Development (AILED) is a research and policy advisory institute transforming how Africa understands and unlocks women’s economic participation.

Across Sub-Saharan Africa, women possess substantial skills yet achieve disproportionately low economic outcomes. In Nigeria alone, this skills–income gap costs an estimated ₦51 trillion in lost GDP annually, representing one of the continent’s largest untapped growth opportunities. Despite billions spent on skills training and empowerment programs, economic returns remain weak.

AILED addresses this failure with rigorous labor economics research and evidence-based policy solutions that correct the structural and market failures undervaluing women’s work.

Our research challenges conventional development approaches by quantifying what is often ignored: information asymmetry in pricing, widespread market saturation in women-dominated sectors, and misalignment between skills acquisition and income generation. Our findings show that pricing correction and market access interventions are up to seven times more effective than traditional training, validating emerging evidence from World Bank and regional studies.

AILED works at the intersection of research, policy advisory, and institutional consulting, supporting governments, multilateral organizations, donors, and corporations. Our work includes labor market assessments, gender wage gap analysis, market saturation studies, program evaluation, and the design of alternative interventions when conventional empowerment models fail.

Founded by Dr. Aisha Yahaya Ndanitsa, a PhD economist specializing in labor economics and gender, AILED combines peer-review quality research with practical implementation support. Our methodologies have demonstrated measurable income improvements by correcting market failures rather than retraining already skilled women.

We do not focus on motivation or skills duplication.
We correct the economic systems

PricelessDAO aspires to become a networked nation-state so that it can host these economic experiments and provide citizenship to its members free of the intervention of the dominant order.

Name: We're Doing This Wrong
Description:
Billions are being wasted on startup programs that create impressive activity but deliver virtually no impact—and DC Cahalane has had enough. 

After two decades building startup ecosystems across three continents and working with tech giants like Google, Microsoft, and NVIDIA on their ecosystem collaborations, Cahalane exposes the uncomfortable truth: we're approaching entrepreneur support completely backwards. Government programs arrive too late, corporate partnerships miss their moment of maximum impact, and entrepreneurs waste precious time navigating fragmented systems designed for industrial-era thinking. 

As AI transforms how companies are built and scaled, this book provides the game-changing "Ecosystem Architecture" framework that moves beyond program proliferation to systematic design of innovation infrastructure. Whether you're a government official frustrated by poor ROI on innovation investment, a corporate leader seeking authentic startup partnerships, or an entrepreneur navigating broken support systems, this blueprint will transform your approach from activity theater to real results. 

The AI revolution is here—architect environments where entrepreneurship thrives, or watch it happen somewhere else.

Available Jan 2026

Name: Cultural Economy
Description: Cultural Economy refers to the economic value generated by cultural assets, creative industries, heritage, and knowledge systems that shape a society’s identity and social fabric. It encompasses sectors such as art, design, media, music, film, crafts, fashion, architecture, heritage tourism, digital content, and indigenous knowledge, while also integrating innovation, technology, and entrepreneurship. By transforming creativity and cultural capital into livelihoods, jobs, and scalable enterprises, the cultural economy plays a critical role in inclusive growth, urban regeneration, and sustainable development—strengthening both economic resilience and soft power in an increasingly globalised world.

Name: Bosansko-h. platforma privrednika u Austriji "BUV"
Description:
MI NISMO OSNOVANI, MI SMO PLOD DRUŽENJA I ŽELJE ZA NAPRETKOM
Udruženje privrednika “Bosnische Unternehmervereinigung – BUV” je oficijelno registrovano 2019. godine u Beču, na inicijativu grupe privrednika bh. porijekla koji žive i posluju u Austriji.

NAŠ STAV JE PREPOZNATLJIV, NAŠ PROGRES JE VIDLJIV
Udruženje se vodi idejom ekonomskog i institucionalnog napretka bh. zajednice u Austriji. Temelji na kojima počiva udruženje su prožeti univerzalnim ljudskim vrijednostima.

Članovi BUV-a žele biti pozitivan primjer čiji principi rada će biti prepoznatljivi po:
- etici poslovanja,
- društvenoj odgovornosti i
- maksimalno profesionalnom pristupu projektima i preuzetim obavezama.
Osim ekonomskih aspekata, udruženje privrednika “BUV” se zalaže za napredak na svim ostalim nivoima koji čine osovinu jednog zdravog društva. Iz tog razloga podržavamo i sami pokrećemo inicijative korisnih društvenih, obrazovnih, socijalnih, kulturoloških, tehnoloških i humanitarnih projekata.

KAO OSNOVNE CILJEVE, IZMEĐU OSTALOG, NAVODIMO:
- jačanje svijesti o važnosti međusobnog upoznavanja, umrežavanja i podrške kako na nacionalnom tako i na internacionalnom nivu između bh. privrednika
zajedničke investicije
- pružanje finansijskog, pravnog i drugog konsaltinga članovima udruženja
podrška pri rješavanju kadrovskih potreba članova
- jačanje svijesti o bitnosti kupovine proizvoda i usluga od kompanija iz BiH kao i onih porijeklom iz BiH.
- Podrška ostalim društveno korisnim projektima.

ŠTA NUDIMO NAŠIM ČLANOVIMA ?
Vodimo se pozitivnim primjerima i idejama i omogućavamo našim članovima mnoštvo beneficija, bitnim kako za uspješno poslovanje tako i za ugodnu atmosferu koja nas prati tokom poslovanja:
– Upoznavanje i umrežavanje – sa ljudima sličnih poslovnih ideja, životnih stavova i principa poslovanja. Pouzdanost, međusobno uvažavanje i društvena odgovornost su uslovi za umrežavanje preko BUV-a.

Name: Asali Organics
Description:
We identify unique and rare products that we have a burning desire to share
with the rest of the world. Asali Organics is a bridge between two worlds — we
provide local tribes and artisans a platform to reach a global audience. Premium raw acacia honey is our first product in the line.

We partner with beekeepers whose instinctive sustainable harvesting traditions and production methods of using simple tools have been handed down over
generations. We are a California-based LLC that is deeply in tune with the devotion of our 5,000 farmers in Kenya who believe everyone deserves to try simple authentic honey.

Why Asali?

Drawn from the wild, untouched lowlands of Africa's Great Rift Valley, our honey is a rare treasure, harvested by the indigenous tribes who have lived in harmony with nature for generations. Each purchase you make directly empowers livelihoods in these marginalized communities. African wild bees forage the thorny acacia that flower for only two weeks a year. Our beekeepers don't have production quotas; they only harvest what is naturally available. 

For every harvest, they leave enough honey in the hive for the brood, to sustain the future generation of the threatened African wild bee.

Name: MX3P
Description:
Fixing What Policy Leaves Behind

Mx3P develops innovative solutions to economic policy failures—especially those governments recognize but struggle to address.

We operate at the intersection of People, Private, and Public systems, designing practical interventions where traditional policy tools fall short. From labor market access and entrepreneurship to care delivery and labor mobility, we work across silos to unlock hidden capacity and deliver real-world results.

X stands for technology. 3P stands for People, Private, and Public—because durable solutions require all three.

We are system and industry builders. Through pilots, partnerships, and policy experimentation, we turn complex challenges into workable models—built to be adopted, adapted, and scaled.

Our work is grounded in research, informed by data, and shaped by experience. We collaborate with employers, governments, and communities to make economic systems more inclusive, responsive, and future-ready.

Learn more at mx3p.com

Name: Communities Squared (C2)
Description:
About C2 

C2 is a community-owned, open-source innovation platform that empowers individuals to transform their ideas into reality. We're on a mission to redefine work by enabling you to embark on your own hero’s journey—turning everyday tasks into meaningful, passion-driven projects. 

Our Vision:

We believe work should be more than just a routine. In a world where many feel disconnected from their jobs , C2 is building an alternative economy where creativity, collaboration, and community lead the way. Our platform provides the tools, resources, and support you need to design a personalized path to success. 

What We Do:

    Empowerment Through Innovation:
    We offer a flexible, open-source platform where you can customize your journey, set your own milestones, and pursue projects that ignite your passion. 

    Community-Driven Development:
    Every feature and update is crafted with input from our community, ensuring C2 evolves to meet the needs of innovators, creators, and forward-thinkers. 

    Collaborative Ecosystem:
    From mentorship and peer support to access to funding and essential tools, C2 connects you with a network that transforms great ideas into impactful outcomes. 

Join the Movement:

We're in our earliest stages and are actively seeking collaborators, beta testers, and contributors to help shape the future of work. Whether you're a developer, entrepreneur, or creative visionary, your input is key to building a platform by the community—for the community. 

Discover a new way to work. Discover C2. 

Feel free to connect, follow, or get in touch to learn more about how you can be a part of this innovative journey!

Name: Zekosoft

Zekosoft är din partner för att förenkla och förbättra företagets fakturering och bokföring.

Vi vet att ekonomihantering kan vara en utmaning för företag av alla storlekar. Därför har vi utvecklat en svit av innovativa programvarulösningar som erbjuder företag och entreprenörer kraftfulla verktyg för fakturering, bokföring, projekthantering och mycket mer.
Med Zekosoft vid din sida kan du fokusera ännu mer på att driva din verksamhet och minska kontorstiden.

Vår resa började med ett enkelt mål: att skapa intuitiva, användarvänliga verktyg som gör ekonomihantering till en bris. Vårt fokus har och kommer alltid vara detsamma - att göra ditt företag mer framgångsrikt genom att erbjuda smartare ekonomilösningar.
Oavsett om du driver ett nystartat företag, är en enskild entreprenör eller har ett större växande företag så har Zekosoft verktygen och expertisen för att förenkla din ekonomihantering och hjälpa dig och ditt företag att nå era mål.
Tack för att du överväger Zekosoft som din ekonomipartner. Tillsammans kan vi skapa en mer framgångsrik och lönsam framtid för ditt företag.

Välkommen till Zekosoft!

The services for which the Timebank mediates range from piano tuning to programming web applications, from bicycle repair to business consultancy. The community has grown into an active network of over 900 users (June 2013), a number that is increasing with 20 to 30 new members every month. Over 1000 registered users
55% users from The Hague
14% from Amsterdam
24% form elsewhere in the Netherlands
7% living abroad

Name: Ohala Africa Foundation
Description:
Ohala Africa is a Kenya-based nonprofit organisation dedicated to advancing inclusive and sustainable economic development by putting people, values, and institutions at the centre of change. Rooted in the Lake Victoria Region and informed by deep local engagement, we work to translate community experience into credible ideas, partnerships, and models that can shape Africa’s economic future.

Our approach recognises that lasting transformation requires more than projects or capital alone. We work collaboratively to inspire collective action, strengthen institutions, and mobilise resources around shared purpose. By aligning people, leadership, and capital within well-governed systems, we help enterprises, producer organisations, and local economies mature and thrive.

Ohala Africa operates at the intersection of thought leadership, convening, policy engagement, and finance-led enterprise development. We actively seek out like-minded partners and forge strategic collaborations that leverage collective insight, credibility, and capability. Through these partnerships, we aim to reduce fragmentation, build trust, and enable long-term value creation across priority economic sectors.

At the heart of our work is a commitment to human dignity and agency. Beyond strengthening markets and institutions, we seek to influence how individuals perceive opportunity, responsibility, and stewardship—recognising that sustainable development ultimately depends on the character, choices, and leadership of people.

Ohala Africa was founded and is led by Pete Ondeng, a development practitioner and thought leader with over three decades of experience in economic development, institutional reform, and strategic advocacy across Africa. Across all our work, we remain grounded in place and guided by purpose—building institutions that endure and opportunities that improve lives.

Name: Digital Regulariza

Atuamos de forma estratégica e operacional para simplificar todo o processo de legalização, cuidando desde a estrutura jurídica e documental até a criação do ecossistema digital do sorteio. Nossa solução é completa: desenvolvemos sites oficiais, criativos para divulgação, estratégias de tráfego pago, acompanhamento técnico e todo o trâmite necessário para que o sorteador possa focar exclusivamente na divulgação do seu projeto.

Com uma abordagem orientada à performance, segurança jurídica e transparência, ajudamos influenciadores, marcas e empresas a realizarem sorteios legais, escaláveis e confiáveis, reduzindo riscos, aumentando credibilidade e potencializando resultados.

A Digital Regulariza une conhecimento legal, marketing digital e execução estratégica para transformar sorteios em operações profissionais, seguras e sustentáveis.

Name: Khoosta
Description:
The story of Khoosta Spaza begins with humble roots, named after a modest stick-and-mud house that became Rodney Landsberg's home during a time of financial hardship. Though simple, this home became a foundation of hope and resilience. From this small space, friends from all walks of life found a place to regroup and pursue their dreams. Many went on to achieve remarkable things—some graduated, others earned PhDs, and some became farmers. Khoosta is a reminder of how far grit and community can take us, and that spirit lives on in our mission.

In 2019, Rodney, along with Mandzisi Dlamini and Vuyani Mokgokong, established Khoosta Spaza in Manzini, initially offering essentials like groceries, airtime, and phone support. Though the shop closed after 11 months due to unforeseen circumstances, it became a trusted source for the community, where relationships grew and everyday needs were met.

Now, with Rodney's recent Master’s degree in Economics, Khoosta Spaza is reimagining itself as a catalyst for local economic empowerment. Our community shops and local producers hold tremendous, untapped promise, even as large, foreign-owned supermarkets dominate. The time has come to uplift our own markets, capitalizing on local potential.

In this next chapter, Khoosta Spaza will become a platform to empower local shop owners and producers, proving that sustainable economic growth starts by investing in our own communities. Together, we can strengthen our local businesses, reduce reliance on international brands, and foster an economy that truly supports the people of Eswatini.

Let’s reimagine local growth, rooted in resilience and community strength.

Name: Talkonomics!!

 We focus on building a strong foundation in introductory micro and macroeconomics through discussion-based learning, real-world application, and collaborative problem-solving.

What We Offer:

• Weekly 1-to-1 virtual sessions
• Microeconomic & Macroeconomic theory breakdowns
• Discussions linking current events to classroom theory
• Exam prep support


Program Timeline:

• Term 1 (Sept–Dec): Microeconomic Fundamentals
• Term 2 (Jan–Apr): Macroeconomic Fundamentals

Talkonomics is built by students, for students. This is a supportive space to explore, ask questions, and build confidence in economics from day one!

Want to book a time for our first session starting the week September 10th?!

Email us at talkonomics.coaching@gmail.com or join our Google Classroom: ocrzlwn2.

Note: Every week there will be 3 spots available for 1 hour 1-on-1 virtual sessions.

Description:

Ich bin Hans Jürgen Waschk, freier Architekt. Mehr als 33 Jahre Erfahrung, spezialisiert auf die ganzheitliche Planung und Umsetzung von Bauprojekten für KMU-Familien-Unternehmen.

Name: Malaysia Students Economics Project
Description:
The Malaysia Students Economics Project (MYSE) is a student-led initiative that advances economic literacy and understanding among secondary school students in Malaysia and across the broader region. Through competitive platforms, educational programs, and research-based learning, we equip students with solid economic knowledge and analytical skills needed to navigate and influence diverse economic landscapes to shape the economies of tomorrow.

Our competitive platforms include economics competitions for students across lower secondary, upper secondary, pre-university, and undergraduate levels in Malaysia. These competitions challenge participants with rigorous exercises combining economic theory with practical application, fostering analytical reasoning, problem-solving skills, and teamwork. We also organize collaborative competitions with partners from Singapore, the United States, and the United Arab Emirates, exposing students to diverse economic perspectives and global best practices to develop comprehensive understanding of interconnected world economies.

Our educational programs encompass specialized economics classes for SPM students, economic policy simulation workshops, data analysis and econometrics bootcamps, and case study competitions focusing on Malaysian economic issues. These initiatives allow students to apply theoretical knowledge to local contexts while building valuable networks for future academic and professional pathways.

Our research initiatives develop analytical capabilities through our Youth Perspectives blog, where Research Associates conduct independent economic analyses on current issues across ASEAN and beyond. Through direct mentorship, these young analysts deepen their exposure to regional economic developments while honing analytical and writing skills, cultivating economists who can contribute to policy debates and sustainable economic growth.

Name: Scottish Pluralism in Economics Conference (SPEC)
Description:
The Scottish Pluralism in Economics Conference (SPEC) is a student-led initiative with a focus on finding solutions to the problems posed by climate change through a pluralistic approach to economics. We believe that neoclassical economics, although widely taught, is limited in its ability to provide answers to the challenges facing the world today. Our goal is to bring together a diverse group of people to explore alternative economic theories and models that can be applied to tackle these problems. 

SPEC will take place in Glasgow on May 27th, 2023 with the theme of "Life on a Finite Planet." This conference is a collaboration between four prestigious Scottish universities - Edinburgh, Glasgow, Dundee, and St Andrews - and is dedicated to addressing the challenges posed by climate change through a pluralistic approach to economics. The conference will bring together policymakers, academics, economists, and students to engage in discussions focused on exploring new and alternative economic theories that offer solutions beyond the confines of neoclassical economics. The theme of "Life on a Finite Planet" emphasises the urgency of finding sustainable solutions to the environmental and social challenges facing humanity, and SPEC will be hosting talks, workshops, and panel discussions to facilitate meaningful conversations and provide a platform for the development of new and innovative solutions.

Name: Selectia Software
Description:
¿Tu contabilidad dice que ganas dinero, pero tu banco dice lo contrario?

Esta es la peligrosa realidad de la inmensa mayoría de las pymes. Se toman decisiones basadas en una contabilidad fiscal que, aunque correcta para Hacienda, omite gastos clave como préstamos, renting o cuotas de autónomos, ocultando la verdadera liquidez del negocio. Esperar al cierre del trimestre para analizar la situación ya no es una opción. Es el momento de elegir entre un diagnóstico precoz o una autopsia cara.

Selectia es el software de control financiero que te da esa visión anticipada. Como empresa partner del Parque Científico de la Universidad Miguel Hernández (UMH), hemos desarrollado un software que traduce la complejidad de tus datos contables en un panel de control intuitivo y visual. Te mostramos la diferencia real entre tu beneficio contable y tu cash flow, permitiéndote tomar decisiones estratégicas basadas en la auténtica salud de tu empresa.

¿Cómo funciona? El proceso es tan sencillo como potente. Sube tu balance de sumas y saldos y, en minutos, tendrás un diagnóstico completo de tu negocio. Nuestra plataforma es ideal para CEOs, Directores Financieros y, especialmente, para asesorías innovadoras que utilizan Holded, ya que facilitamos la integración para que puedan ofrecer un servicio de dirección financiera de alto valor a sus clientes.

Toma las riendas de tu negocio. Síguenos para recibir consejos clave sobre gestión financiera y visita nuestra web en https://selectiasoft.com/ para empezar a transformar tus números en resultados.

Description:

These priorities are shaped by the three highly detailed, research-driven plans that were developed in 2018: the Regional Growth Plan, Talent Marketing Blueprint and Project Wichita. The new direction also takes into account the master plan for downtown’s development, Project Downtown.

Affiliate brands include Wichita Historic Automotive, Choose Broadway and the Auto District Wichita.

Name: New Jobs America
Description:
New Jobs America defends the freedom for any individual to choose to be their own boss.

Sixty million Americans choose to work on their own terms, accommodate their own schedule, and take full responsibility as their own employer.  Their basic economic freedom is under attack by special interests. 

To defend their economic future, New Jobs America researches, writes and promotes federal and state legislation allowing individuals to classify themselves as self-employed. The solution is easier than getting a marriage license.

As a non-profit, New Jobs also researches and publishes original analyses measuring important factors that stimulate rapid job growth, such as work experience and low-capital financial structures. 

New Jobs was founded in 2011 in Massachusetts, origin of the current version of the so-called "ABC Test" in labor law that severely restricts self-employment. We now work nationwide in the US, allied with similar advocates in other English-speaking countries.

Name: USBD Shopping
Description:
At USBD Shopping, we specialize in bringing the vibrant spirit of South Asian culture to South Asian–origin population across the United States. Our mission is simple: to offer the best quality ethnic wear, thoughtfully curated for those who cherish tradition, elegance, and comfort — no matter where they live.

Whether you’re dressing for a family gathering, cultural festival, religious event, or simply expressing your roots with pride, we’re here to make sure you do it in style.

Why Choose USBD Shopping?
We understand what it means to miss home, traditions, and the feeling of belonging. USBD Shopping was created to bridge that gap — to help you carry your culture with you, through fashion that feels familiar and looks phenomenal.

Whether you're in New York, New Jersey, Illinois, Michigan, Florida, Texas, California, or anywhere in US — we're proud to be your trusted source for ethnic fashion that fits your life and your identity.
We’re more than just a shopping destination. We’re a growing community celebrating culture, style, and identity.
Follow us on Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Pinterest, LinkedIn, and X for style tips, new arrivals, and cultural stories.
